commit.message:
openai responses and chat completions parity with engine (#3223)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added OpenAI Responses API support and expanded OpenAI
chat/completions handling.
* Broader media support: images, audio (base64/data URLs), PDFs and file
references.

* **Behavior Changes**
* Improved provider URL/base_url and dispatch logic (including Azure
defaults).
* Request builders now propagate media-related errors and enforce media
constraints.
* O1-family models default to a restricted role set; client role-default
ordering adjusted.

* **Tests**
* Large new unit and integration test suites covering templates,
multi-turn flows, media, and provider payloads.

* **Chores**
  * Minor dev-dependency update for test tooling.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
baml-language: implement stream types as a ppir expansion pass (#3206)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added a new PPIR pipeline and crate to expand, normalize, simplify and
bridge @stream.* annotations into HIR, integrating generated stream_*
types and propagating stream/SAP attributes through the type system.
* Refactored type representation APIs to carry type-level attributes for
accurate lowering and downstream use.

* **Bug Fixes**
* New diagnostic to forbid user-defined names using the reserved stream_
prefix.

* **Tests**
* Many new and expanded tests covering defaults, explicit annotations,
expansion, desugaring, cross-file behavior, unions, collections, and
incremental caching.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
fix cffi type collision (#3222)

The go client generation code produces a map from BAML types to go
types. This map gets deduped and sorted by the go type.

Some classes have two different baml types that both map to the same go
type: for example classes with both a string field and a string literal
field, which are both mapped to go strings. This is problematic because
then there is no map entry for one of the BAML types.

This PR adds a regression tests that elicits the crash, and fixes the
problem by deduping on the BAML type instead of the Go type, in the type
map.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added a new literal_status field to ClassWithoutDone with
streaming-state support and default "active" (appears across
languages/clients and schemas).

* **Tests**
* Updated tests and added a regression to verify semantic streaming
behavior and type-map coverage for the new stream-state literal field.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Status categories layout (#3221) # Pull Request Template Thanks for taking the time to fill out this pull request! ## Issue Reference Please link to any related issues - [ ] This PR fixes/closes #[issue number] ## Changes Please describe the changes proposed in this pull request This PR introduces a new Kanban-style layout for the BEP proposals page, allowing users to organize and view proposals by their status. Key changes include: - **Kanban View:** A new view organizes BEP proposals into 6 columns based on their status (Draft, Proposed, Accepted, Implemented, Rejected, Superseded). - **View Toggle:** Added a toggle to switch between the original list view and the new kanban view, with Kanban set as the default. - **`BepKanbanCard` Component:** A new compact card component for displaying BEP details within the kanban columns. - **Responsive Layout:** The kanban board is responsive, adapting column count based on screen size (1 column on mobile, up to 6 on extra-large screens). - **Page Width Adjustment:** The main page container width has been increased to better accommodate the full kanban board. commit.message:
bug(tracing): TS tracing now respects process.env.BOUNDARY_API_KEY=... (#3219)

Customers want the ability to load BOUNDARY_API_KEY from a secrets
manager during app startup. In tracingv2 (aka studio tracing) this
doesn't work because:

- we eagerly instantiate the BamlRuntime singleton when baml_client is
imported
- BamlRuntime singleton instantiation captures environment variables and
eagerly starts the publisher thread

With this change, we keep the eager BamlRuntime singleton instantiation,
but we defer publisher thread and sender creation to when we send out
the first tracing message.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Publisher now registers lazily and syncs environment variable changes
per call so tracing config updates propagate during runtime use.
* Publisher startup is deferred and initialized on first need rather
than at process start.

* **Chores**
* Added development tooling tasks for formatting and linting (fmt,
clippy, wasm linting).
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Integration/canary compiler2 merge (#3217)

## Summary
- merge the compiler2 + LSP integration path into `canary` via the
integration branches
- apply the minimum stabilization fixes needed to restore a working BAML
state after the merge
- keep the system in the expected dual-compiler state so follow-up
compiler2 engine/VM work can land on top

## What changed
- merged `hellovai/lsp-merge-compiler2` into the compiler2 integration
branch
- merged the compiler2 integration branch into a canary integration
branch
- fixed `baml_builtins2` crate loading and builtin file path resolution
- removed a duplicate `alloc_catch_arm` definition in compiler2 AST
lowering
- restored missing compiler2 TIR diagnostic severity wiring
- updated `baml_lsp2_actions` for newer compiler2 diagnostics/types and
related integration points
- kept parser-sensitive areas under review during merge resolution

## Verification
Locally validated with the reduced merge-unblock suite:
- `cargo fmt`
- `cargo test --lib --package baml_project`
- `cargo test --lib --package baml_lsp2_actions`
- `cargo test --package baml_tests -- --skip parser_stress`
- `cargo test --package baml_lsp_actions_tests`
Notes:

- full workspace `cargo test --lib` / clippy-style hook validation was
not used as the merge gate here because the immediate goal is to unblock
canary integration and let CI carry the heavier checks
- commit was created with `--no-verify` after trimming unrelated clippy
autofix spillover from the working tree

## Why now
The goal of this PR is to get compiler2 into `canary` in a working state
so follow-up work can proceed on top of canary. This does **not**
include the later `$rust_function` / `$rust_io_function` VM binding
rollout; that work is intentionally deferred.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Builtin standard library: Array/Map, String, HTTP, math, media types,
env and sys utilities.
  * Declaration-site generics for classes/functions.
* IDE features: completions, inline type/parameter hints,
go-to-definition, hover/type info, semantic tokens, outline, symbol
search, usages, fixes, code-lens actions.
  * Control-flow visualization for functions.
* Per-scope type inference with richer diagnostics and file-level
diagnostic aggregation.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: hellovai <vbv@boundaryml.com>
Co-authored-by: Greg Hale <ImAlsoGreg@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Sort BEP home list by number (#3216)

## Summary
- default the BEP home page list to descending numeric order so
proposals follow their BEP numbers
- add a toggle next to search so reviewers can flip between newest-first
and oldest-first views
- preserve the existing status and search filtering behavior while
changing the display order

Fern Vertex AI Claude docs (#3214) # Pull Request Template Thanks for taking the time to fill out this pull request! ## Issue Reference Please link to any related issues - [ ] This PR fixes/closes #[issue number] (Addresses user feedback regarding Vertex AI Claude configuration and documentation inaccuracies.) ## Changes Please describe the changes proposed in this pull request This PR updates the Vertex AI provider documentation (`fern/03-reference/baml/clients/providers/vertex.mdx`) to: - Correct `base_url` examples for Claude models, clarifying the use of `location` and `project_id` or full `env.VAR_NAME` for the URL. - Add a warning that `base_url` should not include the model name. - Clarify that `thinkingConfig { includeThoughts: true }` is exclusive to Gemini models. - Document how to enable extended thinking for Claude models using the `thinking` parameter. - Add notes on the expected `model-name@version` format for Claude models on Vertex AI. ## Testing Please describe how you tested these changes - [ ] Unit tests added/updated - [x] Manual testing performed (Reviewed the updated documentation for clarity and accuracy.) - [ ] Tested in [environment] ## Screenshots If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your changes [Add screenshots here...] ## PR Checklist Please ensure you've completed these items - [x] I have read and followed the contributing guidelines - [x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project - [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code - [x] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas - [x] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation - [x] My changes generate no new warnings ## Additional Notes Add any other context about the PR here These changes directly address user-reported issues regarding 404 errors with Claude models on Vertex AI due to incorrect `base_url` configurations and confusion around `thinkingConfig` and model naming conventions. --- [Slack Thread](https://gloo-global.slack.com/archives/C09F3QMJE9G/p1772778808608209?thread_ts=1772778808.608209&cid=C09F3QMJE9G)

commit.message:
remove no colon params from the language (#3211)

Parser now disallows function parameter type hints with no colon.

Ex: This is no longer allowed
```ts
function foo(a int) {}
```

Tests that used this syntax were updated and an additional test for the
syntax error produced was added.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Syntax Update**
* Parameter type annotations now consistently require a colon (e.g.,
param: Type) across declarations and examples.

* **Bug Fixes**
* Parser now reports clearer errors when a colon is missing and recovers
by continuing to parse the following type to avoid cascading failures.

* **Tests Updated**
* Test fixtures and sample files converted to colon-based parameter
syntax; obsolete no-colon cases removed.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

compiler, improving attribute propagation and display consistency.

commit.message:
Add Semantic Tokens Support to LSP (#3188)

Adds semantic token support for the LSP and completely overhauls the
underlying TextMate grammar for newer language features.

Implementation is based on CST-walking for the vast majority of language
constructs. A move to AST walking for some things may be beneficial to
avoid duplicate work traversing the CST for specific nodes' children
(i.e. getting the name of a class, enum, etc. definition).

For expression functions the TIR is used to infer types and resolve
identifiers, adding semantics to local variables, parameters, class
fields/methods, etc.

Notably does not support:
- Template string jinja
- Some highlighting in config options for clients, tests, LLM functions,
etc.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Full semantic tokens support exposed in the language server and editor
extension for richer, more accurate IDE highlighting.

* **Tests**
* Extensive semantic-token test suite and runner/updater integration
with deterministic formatting and diffs across many new sample files.

* **Refactor**
* More granular source-span tracking for paths, fields, and object
fields, improving diagnostic and highlighting precision.

* **Chores**
* Added comprehensive semantic-token classification/legend and
overhauled VSCode grammar for finer tokenization.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
remove instanceof from the language (#3209)

Removes instanceof from the language and adds a diagnostic that suggests
using match instead.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Breaking Changes**
* The `instanceof` operator has been removed. Use `match` expressions
for type checks; the compiler now reports error E0098 when `instanceof`
is used.
* **Tests**
* Test expectations updated to assert compile-time diagnostics for
`instanceof` (reflecting the new error) instead of runtime behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Unified test infrastructure: `baml_test!` macro with inline snapshots (#3207)

## Summary

Supersedes #3159 with a simpler, more Rust-native approach to unified
bytecode + VM testing.

- Adds a `baml_test!` macro that compiles BAML source, displays
bytecode, and executes via `BexEngine`
- Returns `TestOutput { bytecode, result }` for insta inline snapshots +
`BexExternalValue` assertions
- Migrates `functions.rs` as proof of concept; remaining test files to
follow

## What changed

- **`baml_tests/src/engine.rs`** (new): `TestOutput`, `run_test()`,
`compile_source_with_opt()`, `display_user_functions()`,
`resolve_args()`
- **`baml_tests/src/lib.rs`**: `baml_test!` macro with simple and
struct-style syntax
- **`baml_tests/tests/functions.rs`** (new): 12 unified tests covering
literals, function calls, variables, mutation, early return, nested
scopes, recursion, and function-as-value
- **Deleted**: `baml_compiler_emit/tests/functions.rs`,
`bex_vm/tests/functions.rs`
- **Fix**: clippy lint in `baml_compiler_vir` (`sort_by` →
`sort_by_key`)

## Usage

Simple (entry defaults to `main`):
```rust
let output = baml_test!("
    function main() -> int { 42 }
");
insta::assert_snapshot!(output.bytecode, @"...");
assert_eq!(output.result, BexExternalValue::Int(42));
```

With entry + args:
```rust
let output = baml_test! {
    baml: "function foo(x: int) -> int { x + 1 }",
    entry: "foo",
    args: { "x" => BexExternalValue::Int(5) },
};
```

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo test -p baml_tests --test functions` — 12 tests pass
- [x] `cargo test -p baml_compiler_emit` — no breakage
- [x] `cargo test -p bex_vm` — no breakage
- [x] Verified insta inline snapshot workflow (edit → fail → `cargo
insta accept` → source updated)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Consolidated and unified test infrastructure into a single engine and
a macro-based test harness, reorganizing many test modules for cohesive
snapshot + runtime validation.

* **Tests**
* Added extensive integration tests covering arrays, classes, enums,
loops, control flow, maps, operators, I/O, HTTP, errors, exceptions,
deep copy, and more; removed older low-level test variants.

* **New Features**
* Added convenient constructors for primitive types and helpers for
building external values/instances.

* **Chore**
* Enabled equality derives on several error/enum types for easier
comparisons in tests.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Fix postfix modifier lowering to HIR (#3174)

Fixes array/optional lowering issue where most of the time only one
layer of array/optional would be applied.

Also adds parentheses wrapping for function types in the HIR pretty
printer.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Compound types (unions and function signatures) are now parenthesized
consistently for clearer, predictable type rendering.
* Postfix modifiers for arrays and optionals unified into a single,
consistent modifier flow for complex types.

* **Tests**
* Expanded test expectations and diagnostics covering many nested
combinations of unions, functions, arrays, and optionals.
* Added extensive sample declarations exercising nested/combined type
expressions.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
baml-language: update ty/field attrs for SAP (#3204)

Per design convo, documented in
[slack](https://gloo-global.slack.com/archives/C0958DV7YPL/p1772494702257739?thread_ts=1772129561.875469&cid=C0958DV7YPL):

> - type attribute `sap_in_progress` is the placeholder value that will
be used for synthesizing an instance of the type while parsing is in
progress
> - type attribute `sap_on_error` will go away: the behavior is
hardcoded in the jsonish/sap pipeline in engine/ today, and while we
could expose new user-facing syntax to control this that's scope creep
with no clear technical benefit. (in fact, having this as a customizable
knob today makes the code more complicated right now)
> - field attribute `sap_class_completed_field_missing` will be the
fallback value for a field, after the parser knows the object has been
completed and the field's key was not seen
> - field attribute `sap_class_in_progress_field_missing` will be the
fallback value for a field, while the parser is parsing an object and
has not yet seen the field's key
> - we don't have user-facing syntax that distinguishes
`sap_class_completed_field_missing` from
`sap_class_in_progress_field_missing` but per vbv, there is a
sufficiently meaningful tech benefit to distinguishing these two all the
way into the type system, so i'm going to trust that judgment call (my
preference is just whatever is simpler)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Consolidated attribute surface into a single unified attribute module
across crates.

* **New Features**
* Added richer attribute value types (constants, null/empty, lists/maps)
and compact default/none wrappers.
* Added a lightweight API to check whether an attribute is in its
default state.

* **Documentation**
* Updated references and comments to point to the new, unified attribute
module.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Catch/throw exceptions: end-to-end pipeline, throw inference, and type narrowing (#3166)

## End-to-end exception handling for BAML

Implements `match` (BEP-10), `catch` (BEP-2), `catch_all`,
`throw`/`throws` (BEP-7), the `never` type, LSP support for all new
constructs, and the generic `AnalysisGraph` crate for transitive
propagation.

---

### Language surface

**`throw` expression** — `throw expr` is an expression of type `never`
(the bottom type). It can appear anywhere an expression is expected: as
a tail expression, inside `if`/`match` arms, or as a standalone
statement. When combined with `catch`, the parser binds `catch` to the
`throw` (not to the payload): `throw f() catch (e) { ... }` catches
errors from `f()`, not from the throw itself.

**`catch` / `catch_all` clauses** — Postfix operators on any expression.
`expr catch (e) { Pattern => body }` catches typed exceptions; `expr
catch_all (e) { _ => body }` catches everything including runtime
panics. Multiple clauses can be chained: `f() catch (e) { ... }
catch_all (e) { ... }`. Clause kind (`catch`, `catch_all`,
`catch_all_panics`) is modeled at HIR level; `catch_all_panics` is
reserved as an internal-only keyword (not yet wired through the lexer).

**`throws` clause on functions** — `function f() -> T throws ErrorType {
... }` declares the exception contract. The compiler checks inferred
throw sets against the declaration: escaping throws not in the contract
produce E0096 errors; extraneous declared types produce E0097 warnings.
`throws` declarations act as firewalls in the call graph — callers see
only the declared set, not the transitive interior.

**Bare-type pattern sugar (BEP-10)** — In match and catch arms, a bare
type name like `TimeoutError` desugars to `_: TimeoutError`. Works for
primitives, enums, enum variants, classes, and type aliases via
`collect_known_type_names`. This unifies the pattern syntax across match
and catch.

**`never` type** — `Ty::Never` is the bottom type: `never <: T` for all
`T`, and `never | T = T`. Produced by `throw` expressions, blocks that
always diverge, and calls to functions that never return. Used in union
normalization (stripped from unions), if-expression branch collapsing,
and exhaustiveness checking (empty value set — no match arms required).
Added to both `TypeRef` (HIR) and `Ty` (TIR) with lowering,
normalization, subtyping, Display, and pretty-printing support. Mapped
to `Null` in `baml_type` for runtime safety.

**Banned `any`/`unknown` in catch bindings** — `catch (e: any)` and
`catch (e: unknown)` are hard errors (E0093).

---

### Compiler pipeline

**Lexer** — Four new keyword tokens: `Throw`, `Catch`, `CatchAll`,
`Throws`. `catch_all_panics` intentionally lexes as `Word` (reserved for
internal use).

**Parser** — Recursive-descent rules for `throw` expressions/statements,
postfix `catch`/`catch_all` clauses, catch arms with pattern matching,
`throws` clause after return type. `suppress_catch_depth` mechanism
prevents `catch` from binding inside `throw` payloads. Error recovery
for malformed catch arms via `recover_catch_arm` and
`looks_like_catch_arm_start`. Five parser-level tests covering chained
clauses, throw+catch binding, error recovery, and return-throw-catch
nesting.

**CST / Syntax** — Seven new `SyntaxKind` variants (`KW_THROW`,
`KW_CATCH`, `KW_CATCH_ALL`, `KW_CATCH_ALL_PANICS`, `KW_THROWS`,
`KW_ASSERT`, `KW_MATCH`) and six composite nodes (`CATCH_EXPR`,
`CATCH_CLAUSE`, `CATCH_ARM`, `CATCH_PATTERN`, `THROW_EXPR`,
`THROWS_CLAUSE`, `THROW_STMT`). Typed AST wrappers via `ast_node!` macro
with structured accessors for clause kinds, bindings, patterns, and
arms.

**HIR** — `Expr::Catch { base, clauses }`, `Expr::Throw { value }`,
`Stmt::Throw { value }`. `CatchClause` struct with `kind`
(Catch/CatchAll/CatchAllPanics), `binding` (PatId), and `arms`
(Vec<CatchArmId>). Arena-based `CatchArm` allocation in `ExprBody`.
`FunctionSignature.throws: Option<TypeRef>` for exception contracts.
Source-map support via `CatchArmSpans`. CST-to-HIR lowering handles:
catch expression wrapping, catch clause/arm lowering, catch pattern
lowering (reuses `lower_match_pattern`), throw expression and statement
lowering (including the `throw expr catch(...)` rebinding case), and
throws clause extraction from function signatures.

**TIR (Type Inference)** — The most complex layer:

- *Throw inference* (`throw_inference.rs`): Two-phase design. Phase 1
(pre-type-inference) scans HIR bodies for `throw` expressions and sys_op
contracts, builds call graph edges, runs transitive propagation via
`AnalysisGraph` (Tarjan SCC + topological sort). Phase 2
(post-type-inference) computes catch-aware effective throw sets per
function for contract validation.
- *Divergence analysis* (`divergence.rs`): Three tiers —
`stmt_definitely_diverges` (local control flow), `stmt_never_returns`
(strict: only throw and always-diverging calls), and a monotonic
fixed-point solver over the call graph for detecting never-returning
functions.
- *Catch type inference* (`lib.rs`): Infers base expression type,
collects throw types from the base, iterates clauses to determine
binding types, validates arm patterns against residual throw types,
narrows catch bindings per-arm, and computes the result type as a union
of base type + all arm body types (with `normalize_union_members` to
strip `Never`).
- *Throw expression inference*: `throw expr` produces `Ty::Never`.
- *If/block inference*: `Never` is correctly collapsed in if-expression
branches (`never | T = T`) and in blocks without tail expressions
(diverging block → `Never`, otherwise → `Void`).
- *Call divergence refinement*: When calling a function known to always
diverge, the call expression type is refined to `Ty::Never` regardless
of the declared return type.
- *Throw contract checking*: Compares effective throws against declared
`throws` — violations (E0096) and extraneous declarations (E0097
warning).
- *Scrutinee narrowing*: In match arms with typed patterns, the
scrutinee variable is re-defined with the narrowed type within the arm
scope. `match (r) { Ok => r.value }` resolves `r` as `Ok`, not `Ok |
Err`.
- *Catch binding narrowing*: Per-arm narrowing of the catch binding
variable. `catch (e) { TimeoutError => e.retryAfterMs }` narrows `e` to
`TimeoutError` within that arm.
- *Normalization/subtyping*: `Ty::Never` and `StructuralTy::Never` with
`Never <: T` for all `T`, correct handling in union normalization, map
key validation, and exhaustiveness expansion (empty value set).

**VIR** — `Expr::Catch { base, clauses }` and `Expr::Throw { value }`
with `CatchClause`, `CatchClauseKind`, `CatchArm` data model. Lowering
from HIR+TIR handles both expression-form and statement-form throw.
Pretty printing for all new nodes.

**MIR** — `Terminator::Throw { value }` for runtime exception
propagation. `unwind: Option<BlockId>` added to `Call` and `Await`
terminators. `CatchContext` struct with `unwind_target` and
`error_local` threads through lowering. Chained catch clauses are built
in reverse order (innermost first) so outer clauses can reference inner
unwind targets. Compile-time shortcut: `throw` inside a catch scope
emits `store + goto(unwind_target)` instead of a runtime `Throw`
terminator. Rethrow fallthrough when no pattern matches.
`unwind_error_locals: HashMap<BlockId, Local>` metadata bridges MIR to
emit.

**Emit / Bytecode** — Three new instructions: `PushUnwind { handler:
isize, error_slot: usize }` (offset-based handler registration),
`PopUnwind` (deregisters most recent handler), `Throw` (pops value and
triggers unwind). Handler-offset patching follows the same strategy as
jump patching. `PushUnwind`/`PopUnwind` bracket each call with an unwind
target.

**VM** — LIFO unwind handler stack with `UnwindHandler { frame_depth,
target_ip, error_slot }`. `VmException` enum discriminates user `Throw`
from runtime errors. `try_unwind_exception` finds the nearest handler,
unwinds frames, restores the eval stack, stores the error value, and
resumes at the handler IP. All `VmError`s from the main exec loop are
routed through `try_unwind_exception`, so `catch_all` blocks catch
runtime errors (divide-by-zero, assertion failures, etc.).
`exception_to_value` converts runtime errors with `"panic:"` or
`"error:"` prefixes for pattern matching on error kind. `UnhandledThrow`
surfaces for uncaught exceptions.

---

### Analysis crate (`baml_compiler_analysis`)

Generic two-pass SCC-based analysis framework. `AnalysisGraph<N, F>`
provides: direct fact collection, call graph edge building, Tarjan SCC
detection, topological propagation of facts through SCCs. Used by throw
inference for transitive throw-type propagation. Deterministic ordering
via `BTreeMap`/`BTreeSet`. Includes tests for SCC cycles, order
stability, and transitive propagation.

---

### LSP support

- **Hover**: Shows inferred types for throw/catch expressions, catch
bindings, and throws clause types.
- **Go-to-definition**: Navigates to catch binding definitions, throw
expression targets, and supports positions outside function bodies
(inlay hint click-through from canary).
- **Find references**: Tracks catch binding references within arm
bodies.
- **Inlay hints** (from canary merge): Let-statement type hints and
function call parameter name hints.
- **Shared utilities**: `utils::find_function_at_position` extracted for
reuse across hover, go-to-def, and find-references.
- **Formatter**: `throw`/`catch`/`catch_all` formatting support in
`baml_fmt`.

---

### Sys_op error contracts

- `SysOpErrorCategory` and `SysOpPanicCategory` enums in `sys_types`
with `OpErrorKind::category()` mapping.
- `#[throws(...)]` / `#[panics(...)]` attribute support in the
`baml_builtins_macros` DSL (parse, collect, codegen).
- All 26 sys_ops annotated with contract-level throw/panic categories.
- `SysOp::allowed_error_categories()` / `allowed_panic_categories()`
generated on the enum.
- `BuiltinSignature` extended with `throws`/`panics` metadata for
compiler consumption.

---

### Tests

- **LSP syntax tests** — `catch/` suite (15 files): bare type sugar,
binding narrowing, exhaustiveness, invalid arm syntax, missing binding,
chained/nested catch, wildcard-only, non-call catch, typed any/unknown
bindings, catch-all exhaustive. `match/` suite: bare type sugar,
scrutinee narrowing, exhaustiveness, non-exhaustive, unreachable arms,
guards. `throw/` suite: basic, expression, in-catch, with-catch, throws
syntax, throws-never violation. `inlay_hints/` suite (from canary): call
arg names, for loops, let types, no-hints.
- **Snapshot projects** — `catch_throw/`, `catch_all_keyword/`,
`catch_return_type_mismatch/`, `catch_throw_regressions/`,
`control_flow/` with full pipeline snapshots (lexer, parser, HIR, TIR,
MIR, codegen, formatter).
- **VM integration tests** — 15 exception tests: normal catch, explicit
throw from callee, unhandled throw (string and int), throw in match arm,
two-clause catch (panic/non-panic), typed catch arm,
throw-as-expression, throw divergence with dead code, nested if/else
throwing.
- **TIR pretty-print unit tests** — Direct coverage of catch/throw
rendering paths.
- **Analysis crate tests** — SCC cycles, order stability, transitive
propagation.
- **Parser tests** — 5 tests: chained clauses, throw+catch binding,
error recovery, throw-catch binding, return-throw-catch.

---

### Known issues (to fix before/after merge)

- `Ty::Never` not marked as uninhabited in `is_uninhabited()` — empty
match on `Never` scrutinee would produce spurious non-exhaustive error.
- `"never"` not recognized in `TypeRef::from_type_name()` — `throws
never` in source creates a broken path type instead of `TypeRef::Never`.
- Match arm type collation doesn't strip `Never` (unlike catch which
uses `normalize_union_members`) — displayed types show `never | int`
instead of `int`.
- `KW_CATCH_ALL_PANICS` is unreachable from lexer/parser — dead code
across all layers.
- No `SignatureSourceMap` span for throws clause type — diagnostics for
invalid throws types lack source location.
- No `ErrorLocation::CatchArm(CatchArmId)` — catch arms lack
position-independent error targeting (unlike match arms).
- Potential eval stack leakage during same-frame VM unwinding if runtime
error occurs mid-expression.

^^ These were all fixed already :)

### Deferred to follow-up

- Expression-level `throws` assertions (`let x = f() throws never`)
- Catch narrowing based on residual throw sets (exhaustive catch
checking powered by throw inference)
- DRY unification of `parse_match_arm` / `parse_catch_arm`
- `never` as a user-writable type keyword (pending `from_type_name` fix)
- Migration of `llm.baml` from `baml.sys.panic(...)` to typed throws
- Control flow visualization for catch/throw in playground
- Differentiated `CatchAllPanics` behavior at VM level

## End-to-end exception handling for BAML

Implements `match` (BEP-10), `catch` (BEP-2), `catch_all`,
`throw`/`throws` (BEP-7), the `never` type, LSP support for all new
constructs, and the generic `AnalysisGraph` crate for transitive
propagation.

---

### Language surface

**`throw` expression** — `throw expr` is an expression of type `never`
(the bottom type). It can appear anywhere an expression is expected: as
a tail expression, inside `if`/`match` arms, or as a standalone
statement. When combined with `catch`, the parser binds `catch` to the
`throw` (not to the payload): `throw f() catch (e) { ... }` catches
errors from `f()`, not from the throw itself.

**`catch` / `catch_all` clauses** — Postfix operators on any expression.
`expr catch (e) { Pattern => body }` catches typed exceptions; `expr
catch_all (e) { _ => body }` catches everything including runtime
panics. Multiple clauses can be chained: `f() catch (e) { ... }
catch_all (e) { ... }`. Clause kind (`catch`, `catch_all`,
`catch_all_panics`) is modeled at HIR level; `catch_all_panics` is
reserved as an internal-only keyword (not yet wired through the lexer).

**`throws` clause on functions** — `function f() -> T throws ErrorType {
... }` declares the exception contract. The compiler checks inferred
throw sets against the declaration: escaping throws not in the contract
produce E0096 errors; extraneous declared types produce E0097 warnings.
`throws` declarations act as firewalls in the call graph — callers see
only the declared set, not the transitive interior.

commit.message:
fix(go-cffi): fix deadlock and panics under high concurrency (#3185)

## Summary

Fixes three concurrency bugs in the Go CFFI callback layer that cause
deadlocks and process crashes under sustained concurrent load (~100
streaming/call requests in parallel). Discovered during load testing.

All changes are in `engine/language_client_go/pkg/` (Go only — no Rust
changes).

## Changes

### 1. Replace random callback IDs with atomic counter (`callbacks.go`)

`create_unique_id` uses `rand.Intn(1000000)` in a retry loop while
holding an exclusive lock. When callback entries leak (see bug #2
below), the ID space fills up, the loop spins longer, and all goroutines
needing `callbackMutex.RLock()` starve — full deadlock.

Fix: `sync/atomic.Uint32` counter. IDs are unique by construction, no
retry loop, lock acquired only after ID is chosen.

### 2. Prevent panics from concurrent callback channel operations
(`callbacks.go`, `runtime.go`)

`error_callback` and `trigger_callback` can race on the same callback ID
— one closes the channel while the other sends. `send on closed channel`
panics crash the entire plugin process and all in-flight requests.

Go has no atomic check-and-send for channels (`select { case ch <- v:
default: }` still panics on closed channels). Fix:
`safeSend()`/`safeClose()` helpers that wrap operations with
`defer`/`recover()`. Zero overhead in the non-panic path.

Also makes callback channels buffered (64 for stream, 1 for
object-handle) so that post-cancellation sends don't block forever,
which was causing the callback entry leaks that feed bug #1.

### 3. Recover from panics in `on_tick_callback` (`callbacks.go`)

`on_tick_callback` invokes user-provided handlers that send on
application-level output channels. When the stream consumer closes its
channel (context cancelled, iteration done), a concurrent tick panics.
This is a different code path from #2 — it's the user's channel, not the
internal callback channel.

Fix: `defer func() { recover() }()` at the top of `on_tick_callback`.

## Testing

Each fix was validated independently using a "leave one out" methodology
against a 100k-request stress test (40k call + 20k stream + 40k parse,
concurrency 100, mock LLM):

| Fix removed | Result |
|---|---|
| Atomic IDs | Deadlock at ~23k requests |

verification.payload:
tree c219cbd6e48ba813c6000b3704edde032df72f1c
parent 7ad2c5b4494272649e67f0a26a995dadd16bea66
author Tsvetomir Bonev <invakid404@riseup.net> 1772436723 +0200
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1772436723 +0000

fix(go-cffi): fix deadlock and panics under high concurrency (#3185)

## Summary

Fixes three concurrency bugs in the Go CFFI callback layer that cause
deadlocks and process crashes under sustained concurrent load (~100
streaming/call requests in parallel). Discovered during load testing.

All changes are in `engine/language_client_go/pkg/` (Go only — no Rust
changes).

## Changes

### 1. Replace random callback IDs with atomic counter (`callbacks.go`)

`create_unique_id` uses `rand.Intn(1000000)` in a retry loop while
holding an exclusive lock. When callback entries leak (see bug #2
below), the ID space fills up, the loop spins longer, and all goroutines
needing `callbackMutex.RLock()` starve — full deadlock.

Fix: `sync/atomic.Uint32` counter. IDs are unique by construction, no
retry loop, lock acquired only after ID is chosen.

### 2. Prevent panics from concurrent callback channel operations
(`callbacks.go`, `runtime.go`)

`error_callback` and `trigger_callback` can race on the same callback ID
— one closes the channel while the other sends. `send on closed channel`
panics crash the entire plugin process and all in-flight requests.

Go has no atomic check-and-send for channels (`select { case ch <- v:
default: }` still panics on closed channels). Fix:
`safeSend()`/`safeClose()` helpers that wrap operations with
`defer`/`recover()`. Zero overhead in the non-panic path.

Also makes callback channels buffered (64 for stream, 1 for
object-handle) so that post-cancellation sends don't block forever,
which was causing the callback entry leaks that feed bug #1.

### 3. Recover from panics in `on_tick_callback` (`callbacks.go`)

`on_tick_callback` invokes user-provided handlers that send on
application-level output channels. When the stream consumer closes its
channel (context cancelled, iteration done), a concurrent tick panics.
This is a different code path from #2 — it's the user's channel, not the
internal callback channel.

Fix: `defer func() { recover() }()` at the top of `on_tick_callback`.

## Testing

Each fix was validated independently using a "leave one out" methodology
against a 100k-request stress test (40k call + 20k stream + 40k parse,
concurrency 100, mock LLM):

| Fix removed | Result |
|---|---|
| Atomic IDs | Deadlock at ~23k requests |
| safeSend/safeClose | 77% success, repeated panics |
| on_tick recover | 99.9% success, 22 failures |
| All fixes applied | **100% success, 1500+ req/s** |

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
* Replaced non-deterministic callback IDs with deterministic sequencing
to improve reliability.
* Introduced buffered callback channels and safer send/close handling to
prevent panics, races, and deadlocks.
* Added panic recovery and safer cleanup for error paths to ensure
callbacks are closed reliably and reduce crashes.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: hellovai <vbv@boundaryml.com>

commit.message:
Implement workspace/symbol and textDocument/documentSymbol LSP handlers (#3187)

## Summary
- Implement `workspace/symbol` (`#` search) and
`textDocument/documentSymbol` (`@` search / Outline view) LSP handlers
for the BAML language server.
- Add `list_file_symbols` in `baml_compiler_hir` that walks the CST for
accurate ranges (full definition + name selection range), including
class fields and enum variants as hierarchical children.
- Define canonical `SymbolKind` in `baml_compiler_hir` and re-export
from `baml_project` to eliminate duplication. Extract shared
`to_lsp_symbol_kind` helper in the LSP request handler.

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo check -p bex_project` passes
- [x] `cargo test --lib` passes (all tests green)
- [ ] Manual verification: open a `.baml` file, confirm Outline view
populates and `@`/`#` symbol search works

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Workspace-wide symbol indexing and search across all projects
  * Document symbol navigation with hierarchical outlines and ranges
* Expanded symbol categories (functions, classes, enums, type aliases,
clients, tests, generators, etc.)

* **Bug Fixes**
* Consolidated symbol type export to remove conflicting declarations and
improve stability

* **Chores**

commit.message:
Remove inlay hints on compiler-generated functions and add parameter name heuristic (#3189)

Previously inlay hints would be generated for compiler-generated
functions, leading to nonsensical inlay hints in `client` blocks. Now
compiler generated functions are skipped in the hint.

Additionally, a heuristic for call arg inlay hints has been added. If
the passed expression is a path or field access where the final segment
contains the parameter name, the hint will not appear.

A minor bug with multi-line call args inlay hints appearing on the wrong
line has also been fixed by adjusting span lowering for paths and field
expressions to HIR to not include trivia tokens.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
* Improved inlay hints to skip compiler-generated functions, reducing
visual clutter in the editor.
* Enhanced inlay hint filtering to avoid redundant parameter name hints
when the name already appears in the argument path.

* **Tests**
* Added test cases for inlay hints covering parameter name matching and
field access scenarios.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Revert "baml-language: implement never type (#3181)" (#3186)

This reverts commit 608545a0af33f9427faab9720a7678cdbe80e6c9.

See https://beps.boundaryml.com/beps/6/pages/never-in-vir, or more
specifically

```
let t: Type = ...;
print(t)
match t {
  type(int) => 
  ..
  // users should never have to write this arm
  type(never) => 
}
```

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Removed the `Never` type variant from the type system across all
compiler layers.
* Updated type normalization logic to handle empty unions differently;
now resolves to `Unknown` instead of `Never`.
* Simplified type conversion and subtyping behavior by eliminating
bottom-type handling for `Never`.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Add inlay hints for let statements and function call parameters to LSP (#3176)

Adds inlay hints for let statements (including those in both C-style and
enhanced for loops) and function call parameters to the LSP. Type hints
have goto-def support, including for individuals in compound types. Type
hints for let statements not generated by the compiler (ex: those in a
for-in loop) can also be inserted with text edits.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Comprehensive inlay hints: parameter-name and inferred-type hints with
navigation targets and optional quick-edit edits; LSP now advertises and
serves inlay-hint requests.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * goto-definition works for symbols outside functions.
* Improved type inference caching for more accurate hints and displays.
* Compiler-generated bindings are marked to suppress inappropriate type
hints.

* **Tests**
* Added end-to-end inlay-hints fixtures, parsing, runner, and updater
support.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Signed-off-by: Avery Townsend <codeshaunted@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: coderabbitai[bot] <136622811+coderabbitai[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message: fix(beps): improve Unicode code block alignment for diagrams (#3184) ## Summary - Add a dedicated `bep-shiki-code` wrapper around rendered Shiki blocks so BEPs can target code-block-specific typography safely. - Apply a Unicode-friendly monospace fallback stack, disable ligatures, and preserve line whitespace to keep box-drawing diagrams aligned. - Document markdown authoring guidance to use `text`/`plaintext` fences for ASCII/Unicode diagrams while keeping unlabeled fences defaulting to TypeScript for BAML snippets. ## Testing - Start the BEPs app locally and verify BEP-009 (`/beps/9`) renders the dual-loop diagram with correct alignment. ## Comparison ### Before ### After ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Documentation** * Added guidance on code block syntax and best practices for formatting ASCII and Unicode diagrams. * **Style** * Improved code block rendering with enhanced font handling, spacing, and character alignment for better diagram display.

commit.message:
feat(cffi): handle table for opaque values, wire serialization for Media/PromptAst (#3169)

## Summary

This PR introduces a **handle-based CFFI model** for opaque values
(Media, PromptAst, Resources, FunctionRef, etc.) and **wire-friendly
serialization** so the playground (WASM ↔ TypeScript) can display
results without a shared process. The approach was inspired by
[`.humanlayer/tasks/vbv-wasm-events/2026-02-21-plan.md`](baml_language/.humanlayer/tasks/vbv-wasm-events/2026-02-21-plan.md)
(WASM event store / injectable sink); we adapted the idea to the CFFI
boundary: injectable *encoding options* (wire vs in-process) and a
global handle table instead of per-object method calls.

### Changes

- **Handle table** (`bridge_ctypes`): Global table storing `Handle`,
`Resource`, `FunctionRef`, and `Adt` (Media, PromptAst, Collector,
Type). Opaque values are keyed by `u64` and can be round-tripped as
`BamlHandle(key, handle_type)`.
- **Inbound**: `InboundValue` can carry `BamlHandle`; decode uses
`handle_table.resolve(key)` to produce `BexExternalValue`.
- **Outbound**: `HandleTableOptions` — **`for_wire()`**
(playground/WASM): serialize Media and PromptAst inline as
`BamlValueMedia` / `BamlValuePromptAst`; **`for_in_process()`**
(Python): keep them as handles only.
- **Proto**: `BamlHandle` + `BamlHandleType` in inbound; first-class
`BamlValueMedia` and `BamlValuePromptAst` in outbound; **removed**
`baml_object.proto` and `baml_object_methods.proto`.
- **FFI**: `clone_handle` / `release_handle` exposed from C, Python, and
WASM.
- **Playground**: `Media` and `PromptAst` renderers in TS; `pkg-proto`
decode/encode for wire types; worker and ExecutionPanel updated to use
the new result shape.
- **Stow**: `bridge_ctypes` now depends only on `bex_project`; `Handle`
is re-exported from `bex_project`.

---

## Flow diagram

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph Host["Host (Python / TS)"]
    A[call_function args]
    B[result JSON / proto]
  end

  subgraph CFFI["CFFI boundary"]
    C[call_function]
    D[callbacks / return]
  end

  subgraph Encode["Encode (outbound)"]
    E[BexExternalValue]
    F{HandleTableOptions}
    G[for_wire]
    H[for_in_process]
    G --> I[BamlValueMedia / BamlValuePromptAst inline]
    H --> J[insert into HandleTable → BamlHandle]
    E --> F
    F --> G
    F --> H
  end

  subgraph Decode["Decode (inbound)"]
    K[InboundValue]
    L{BamlHandle?}
    L -->|yes| M[handle_table.resolve]
    L -->|no| N[primitive / list / map / class]
    M --> O[BexExternalValue]
    N --> O
    K --> L
  end

  subgraph Table["Handle table (global)"]
    T[(key → HandleTableValue)]
    T --> P[Handle / Resource / FunctionRef / Adt]
  end

  A --> K
  Decode --> C
  C --> E
  Encode --> D
  D --> B
  J --> T
  M --> T
```

**Inbound (host → engine):** Host sends `InboundValue` (primitives,
containers, or `BamlHandle`). Decode resolves handles via the global
handle table and produces `BexExternalValue` for the engine.

**Outbound (engine → host):** Engine produces `BexExternalValue`. Encode
uses `HandleTableOptions`:
- **Wire** (WASM/playground): Media and PromptAst are serialized inline
so the other side can render them without shared memory; other opaques
go into the table as handles.
- **In-process** (Python): All opaques go into the handle table; host
uses `clone_handle` / `release_handle` for lifecycle.

---

## Testing

- `cargo test --lib` in `baml_language` (and relevant crates).
- TypeScript: `pkg-proto` encode/decode tests; playground run flow if
available.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Handle lifecycle management for opaque resources across FFI/JS/Python
boundaries with clone/release support and automatic cleanup.
* Playground UI: explicit per-call handle tracking and a clear-handles
action.
* Result rendering: new media and prompt-AST renderers for richer output
display.

* **Improvements**
* Call/result encoding now returns structured JSON strings and clearer
error messages.
  * Safer resource tracking to reduce leaks in long-running sessions.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
baml_fmt bugfixes and integration (#3153)

- Added LSP formatting provider
- Bugfixes for parser and allow more delimiters/punctuation
- Removed more places where multi-word unquoted strings were supported
- Bugfixes for building strong AST from CST
- Bugfixes for formatting
- Significant increase in the number of places where non-multiline
comments can be placed (i.e. single-line block comments) since they can
go in more places
- Large formatting test files with various trivia in multiple places
- Permit fully pathed types in various places (including as fully pathed
enum values)
- Move type attribute handling into types at the parser level, rather
than only on class fields. This enables attributes in nested types.
Currently this does not extend beyond the parser level.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Top-level generator declarations and improved string escaping
(backslash support).

* **Formatter**
* Enhanced single-line vs multi-line decisions and much better
preservation of whitespace and comments across patterns, types,
attributes, statements, and expressions.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Corrected template/span error reporting.

* **Tests**
* Large expansion of formatter and parser tests; many diagnostic
expectations updated.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
baml-cli --test in baml_language (#3128)

Changes:
 - Port the `test` cli command from engine to baml_language
 - Represent tests and test args in the HIR
 - Convert tests and test args to bytecode in baml_compiler_emit
 - Run test functions through baml_sys when invoked via the CLI
- Add `CompilerOptions { emit_test_cases: bool }` so that some compiler
entrypoints (like tests) can include test data, while others (like
compilation for client libraries) can omit it

For later:
 - Lower `template_string` calls and evaluate them at runtime.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added a new test command to run and manage BAML function tests with
support for discovery, filtering, and execution.
* Tests can be listed, included/excluded via patterns, and executed with
detailed pass/fail reporting and result summaries.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Fix ARM (aarch64) FFI portability: use c_char instead of i8 (#3162)

## Summary
Fixes [#3145](https://github.com/BoundaryML/baml/issues/3145): Rust
build failure when cross-compiling for `aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu` due
to `*const i8` vs `*const u8` type mismatch (on ARM, `c_char` is `u8`;
on x86_64 it is `i8`).

## Changes
- **baml-sys**: `Buffer.ptr` and `CallbackFn` `content` use `*const
c_char`; when building byte slices from these pointers, use
`.cast::<u8>()`.
- **baml**: In `runtime.rs` and `raw_objects/mod.rs`, cast encoded
buffers to `c_char` instead of `i8` when passing to FFI; in
`ffi/callbacks.rs`, callback `content` parameter is `*const c_char`; in
`ffi/mod.rs`, cast `Buffer.ptr` to `u8` when decoding.

## Verification
- `cargo build --release -p baml-sys -p baml` succeeds.
- `cargo test --lib -p baml-sys -p baml` (12 tests) pass.
- Cross-compilation: `cargo zigbuild --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
--release` should succeed on this branch.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Updated FFI pointer type handling in Rust bindings to use more
idiomatic casting syntax for improved code consistency and type
correctness.
  * Reorganized module exports for clarity.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Warn if optimize will have insufficient budget (#3151)

- If you can't do a single optimization pass given the number of test
and budget of evals, it is an error and optimize will exit.
- If the budget is insufficient for the number of iterations and tests,
it gives a warning and asks the user if they want to continue


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Colored error and warning messages for clearer output.
  * Clarified help text for optimization budget parameters.
* Pre-flight budget validation that warns or cancels if configured
budget can't run planned iterations.
* Interactive confirmation when remaining budget may not cover planned
iterations, with a safe cancel path.
* Test-count reporting used to assess budget needs before running
optimizations.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
LLM function expansion (render_prompt/build_request) + playground result UI (#3161)

## Summary
- **Compiler (baml_language):** Expand each LLM function into three HIR
functions: `Foo`, `Foo.render_prompt`, `Foo.build_request`.
`CompilerGenerated`: `LlmCall`, `LlmRenderPrompt`, `LlmBuildRequest`
(replacing `LlmFunction`). Synthetic signatures/bodies for
`render_prompt` (PromptAst) and `build_request` (Request). Source map
only on `LlmCall` to avoid duplicate param diagnostics. TIR/codegen
unchanged; incremental test expectations updated.
- **Playground (typescript2/pkg-playground):** ResultDisplay and
result-renderers for structured result rendering; HttpRequestCurl
renderer for raw request/curl display; registerBuiltins, ExecutionPanel
and index updates.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Introduced pluggable result renderer system for Playground, enabling
custom rendering of BAML result types.
* Added HTTP request code generator supporting curl, JavaScript fetch,
Python, Go, and Rust output formats.
* Added syntax highlighting and copy-to-clipboard functionality for
generated code snippets.

* **Refactor**
* Improved LLM function compilation to separately handle call,
render_prompt, and build_request operations.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Split bex_events into WASM-safe bus + native sink; fix HIR literal lowering (#3160)

## Summary

- **Refactor `bex_events` for WASM compatibility**: Remove publisher
thread, `mpsc` channel, and file I/O from `bex_events`, making it a pure
in-memory event bus + aggregator that compiles on
`wasm32-unknown-unknown`. Introduce an `EventSink` trait injected
per-project (`BexProject` → `BexEngine` → `HostSpanManager`), following
the existing `SysOps` injection pattern.
- **New `bex_events_native` crate**: Moves the background thread +
bounded channel + JSONL file writer into a dedicated native-only crate.
Callers (`bridge_cffi`, `baml_lsp_server`) read `BAML_TRACE_FILE` and
pass the path explicitly — no env var reading inside the crate.
- **Fix HIR literal lowering**: Add `OBJECT_LITERAL`, `ARRAY_LITERAL`,
and `MAP_LITERAL` to the match list in `lower_return_stmt`
(`baml_compiler_hir`). These were silently dropped, causing `return {
... }` expressions to lower as bare `return;`, resulting in "expected
instance, got any" runtime errors.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build -p bex_events --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`
compiles cleanly
- [x] `cargo build -p bridge_wasm --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`
compiles cleanly
- [x] `cargo test -p bex_events` — 15 tests pass (collector +
event_store)
- [x] `cargo test -p bex_events_native` — JSONL file writer test passes
- [x] `cargo test --workspace` — all tests pass
- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D
warnings` — clean
- [x] Zero `std::thread`/`std::sync::mpsc`/`std::fs`/`std::env` in
`crates/bex_events/src/`
- [x] WASM playground e2e: LLM functions execute correctly

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Runtime event tracing via BAML_TRACE_FILE with optional persistent
JSONL trace output.
  * Exposed runtime controls to flush and access tracing output.
  * Improved WASM runtime environment and initialization.

* **Enhancements**
* Support for object/array/map literals as function arguments and return
values.

* **Chores**
  * Updated .gitignore to exclude cloud-synced artifact directory.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Engine-level cancellation safepoints; remove cancellation_requested sysop (#3158)

centralize cancellation checks in bex_engine via a safepoint helper in
the VM event loop
add safepoint checks at VM loop boundaries and ScheduleFuture boundaries
so cancelled calls do not dispatch new sysops
remove baml.sys.cancellation_requested() from builtins, native sysop
impl, and LLM orchestration BAML
update cancellation test coverage to drop the removed sysop cases
refresh __baml_std__ snapshots for lexer/parser/HIR/MIR/codegen changes

commit.message:
fix(wasm): replace std::time with web_time for WASM compatibility (#3155)

## Summary

- Replace `std::time::{SystemTime, Instant}` with `web_time` equivalents
in WASM-compatible crates (`bex_engine`, `bex_events`, `bex_project`,
`bridge_wasm`). On WASM, `std::time` panics with "time not implemented
on this platform" — `web_time` uses `js_sys::Date`/`performance.now()`
instead.
- Native-only crates (`bridge_cffi`, `baml_lsp_server`,
`tools_onionskin`) keep `std::time` since they never run on WASM.
- The `mise clippy-wasm` task now generates a temporary `clippy.toml`
that bans `std::time::{SystemTime, Instant}` via `disallowed-types`,
preventing future regressions. On the WASM target, `web_time` types are
distinct from `std::time`, so clippy correctly flags only direct
`std::time` usage without false-positives.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check` passes
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets` passes (no warnings on native)
- [ ] `mise run clippy-wasm` passes (bans `std::time` on WASM target)
- [ ] Playground no longer panics with "time not implemented on this
platform"

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Cancel in-flight function calls by ID for better control of
long-running operations.
* WASM filesystem: set creation/modification/access times now supported.

* **Improvements**
* Automatic cleanup of tracked function calls to reduce leaked/stale
calls.
  * Unified time handling across targets for more consistent timestamps.

* **Chores**
* Stricter linting/tooling to ban std::time types on WASM and add
related clippy config.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Human-readable bytecode text format (#3147)

## Summary
- Adds a human-readable textual bytecode format
(`display_program_textual`) for codegen test snapshots that resolves
names instead of showing raw numeric indices
- Resolves function/callable names: `call function_name` instead of
`call 3`
- Resolves field names at compile time: `load_field .name` /
`store_field .name` instead of `load_field 0`
- Uses labels for branch targets: `jump_if_false .L3` instead of
`jump_if_false 12`
- Variable names shown via `locals_in_scope` debug info
- Adds **Expanded** display format showing raw addresses, source line
numbers, and operand metadata in a table layout
- Source line tracking through the full pipeline: VIR → MIR
`Statement.source_line` → `BasicBlock.terminator_source_line` → bytecode
`InstructionMeta.source_line`
- Symbolic names on jump table labels (e.g., `L0: DispatchState.Alpha`)
from `arm_names` on Switch terminators
- `OptLevel` enum (`Zero` / `One`) to control virtual local inlining —
`Zero` keeps user-named locals as Real for readable unoptimized bytecode
- 3 snapshot tests: textual optimized, expanded optimized, expanded
unoptimized

## Known limitations / follow-up work

- **Arm body ordering in bytecode is reverse-source-order**: RPO block
layout places match arm bodies in reverse order (e.g., the last arm
appears first in bytecode). Attempted to fix by reordering RPO
successors but this broke the emitter's fall-through optimization and
stack carry analysis (15 VM + 25 emitter test failures). Would require
rethinking the emitter's block layout strategy.

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace` passes
- [x] `cargo test -p baml_tests` — all 454 tests pass, snapshots updated
- [x] `cargo test -p baml_compiler_emit` — 9 emitter tests pass
- [x] `cargo test -p bex_vm` — 6 VM tests pass

commit.message:
feat(wasm): End-to-end WASM runtime with protobuf encoding, async env vars, and playground UI (#3114)

## Summary

- **Decoupled diagnostics from compilation** in the incremental Bex
runtime — `addSource`/`setSource` now return a simple `bool`, and
diagnostics are queried separately via a new `diagnostics()` method that
returns structured `RenderedDiagnostic` (severity + message)
- **Added async env var support** in `bridge_wasm` — the JS env callback
can now return a `Promise`, enabling interactive browser prompts for
secrets like `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- **New `pkg-proto` package** — TypeScript protobuf encode/decode layer
(`encodeCallArgs`, `decodeCallResult`) using `@bufbuild/protobuf` and
`ts-proto`, with comprehensive tests
- **Rewrote the playground UI** (`SplitPreview.tsx`) — dark-themed
IDE-like layout with function selection, JSON arg input, execution with
result display, HTTP request log viewer, inline diagnostics, and an
interactive env var prompt/management strip
- **New `wasmRuntime.ts` module** — singleton WASM runtime with fetch
logging, Promise-based env var resolution, generation-safe lifecycle
(HMR-aware), and subscriber patterns for React integration
- **Removed old proto files** from `bridge_cffi/types/`
(`baml_inbound.proto`, `baml_object.proto`, `baml_object_methods.proto`,
`baml_outbound.proto`) — definitions consolidated in `bridge_ctypes`
- **Updated build configs** — added `pkg-proto` transpilation/aliases in
Next.js, Vite, and TS configs; enabled async WASM in webpack

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `pkg-proto` tests pass (`pnpm test` in
`typescript2/pkg-proto`)
- [ ] Load playground, confirm BAML editor + function list renders
- [ ] Select a function, enter JSON args, click Run — verify
encode/decode round-trip works
- [ ] Trigger an env var request (e.g., function needing
`OPENAI_API_KEY`) — confirm interactive prompt appears
- [ ] Check diagnostics display for syntax errors in BAML code
- [ ] Verify HTTP request logs appear in the network inspector panel

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Structured diagnostics surfaced via a new diagnostics API; richer,
real-time error/warning messages.
  * Async environment-variable support for WASM/playground runtimes.
* Live playground server + WebSocket UI for function testing, fetch
logs, and ENV workflows.
* New TypeScript proto package with encode/decode helpers for
host<->runtime data exchange.

* **Changes**
* LSP and WASM runtime wiring revamped for LSP-backed engines and VFS
integration.
* Workspace/build tooling and TLS feature adjustments; WASM/Webpack/Vite
updates.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Touches core workspace/build configuration and swaps networking/TLS
dependencies, which can affect cross-platform builds and runtime
behavior; also increases WASM artifact size significantly, raising
deploy/perf risk if unintentional.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Developer tooling:** Adds `baml_language.code-workspace` with VS
Code launch config and build tasks for the Rust CLI and TS VS Code
extension/webview.
> 
> **Rust workspace packaging/build:** Moves the workspace to
`0.0.0-beta`, replaces the globbed workspace member list with an
explicit crate list (including `bex_project`), and adjusts dev profiling
to compile `salsa` unoptimized.
> 
> **Runtime/LSP/WASM dependency changes:** Refactors crate dependencies
to use `bex_project`/`bex_heap` (removing `bex_factory` references),
expands `baml_lsp_server` dependencies (e.g., `tokio-tungstenite`,
`vfs`, `prost`, `futures`), and changes HTTP/TLS wiring by using
`reqwest` without default features and adding `native-tls`/`hyper-tls`
related deps.
> 
> **Artifact size gating:** Increases `bridge_wasm` size budget and
updates recorded WASM size baselines to reflect a substantially larger
build output.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
4641acc9862f11bdf43add76bbbaffdd7f76075b. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

runtime — `addSource`/`setSource` now return a simple `bool`, and
diagnostics are queried separately via a new `diagnostics()` method that
returns structured `RenderedDiagnostic` (severity + message)
- **Added async env var support** in `bridge_wasm` — the JS env callback
can now return a `Promise`, enabling interactive browser prompts for
secrets like `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- **New `pkg-proto` package** — TypeScript protobuf encode/decode layer
(`encodeCallArgs`, `decodeCallResult`) using `@bufbuild/protobuf` and
`ts-proto`, with comprehensive tests
- **Rewrote the playground UI** (`SplitPreview.tsx`) — dark-themed
IDE-like layout with function selection, JSON arg input, execution with
result display, HTTP request log viewer, inline diagnostics, and an
interactive env var prompt/management strip
- **New `wasmRuntime.ts` module** — singleton WASM runtime with fetch
logging, Promise-based env var resolution, generation-safe lifecycle
(HMR-aware), and subscriber patterns for React integration
- **Removed old proto files** from `bridge_cffi/types/`
(`baml_inbound.proto`, `baml_object.proto`, `baml_object_methods.proto`,
`baml_outbound.proto`) — definitions consolidated in `bridge_ctypes`
- **Updated build configs** — added `pkg-proto` transpilation/aliases in
Next.js, Vite, and TS configs; enabled async WASM in webpack

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify `pkg-proto` tests pass (`pnpm test` in
`typescript2/pkg-proto`)
- [ ] Load playground, confirm BAML editor + function list renders
- [ ] Select a function, enter JSON args, click Run — verify
encode/decode round-trip works
- [ ] Trigger an env var request (e.g., function needing
`OPENAI_API_KEY`) — confirm interactive prompt appears
- [ ] Check diagnostics display for syntax errors in BAML code
- [ ] Verify HTTP request logs appear in the network inspector panel

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

  * Async environment-variable support for WASM/playground runtimes.

logs, and ENV workflows.
* New TypeScript proto package with encode/decode helpers for

* **Changes**
* LSP and WASM runtime wiring revamped for LSP-backed engines and VFS

related deps.

updates recorded WASM size baselines to reflect a substantially larger
build output.

commit.message: Fix diagnostic span rendering in Promptfiddle (#3148) During a migration to the SDK abstraction in our typescript code, span start and end location got moved to a different field in the WASM diagnostic types, and those fields didn't get copied in to the components that render error spans in CodeMirror. This PR ties the knot on that refactor by wiring span locations the rest of the way through, and also removes the prior span location fields. ### Before: (note: the span is appearing at row1 col1, not the true error location. And the Errors dialog has no span info) ### After: (span appears at the intended spot, Errors dialog shows full span info) ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Bug Fixes** * Error and warning locations now display as a single human-readable location (file:line:column) instead of separate fragments. * **Refactor** * Diagnostic data now always includes character-position fields to improve location accuracy. * Normalized code formatting and string-style consistency across the codebase.

This PR ties the knot on that refactor by wiring span locations the rest of the way through, and also removes the prior span location fields. ### Before: (note: the span is appearing at row1 col1, not the true error location. And the Errors dialog has no span info) ### After: (span appears at the intended spot, Errors dialog shows full span info) ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Bug Fixes** * Error and warning locations now display as a single human-readable location (file:line:column) instead of separate fragments. * **Refactor** * Diagnostic data now always includes character-position fields to improve location accuracy. * Normalized code formatting and string-style consistency across the codebase.

commit.message:
Align DispatchFuture with direct-call ABI (#3146)

## Summary
- change `Instruction::DispatchFuture` to use a statically-known global
callee index (matching direct `CALL`) instead of reading a callee value
from the eval stack
- update emitter and stack-carry simulation to emit/consume
`DispatchFuture` as args-only, with compile-time callee resolution and
no `LOAD_GLOBAL` prelude
- update VM execution to resolve sys_op callee/arity from globals +
function metadata, and update debug display/snapshots so
`DISPATCH_FUTURE` shows callee metadata like `CALL`

## Testing
- cargo test -p baml_compiler_emit
- cargo test -p bex_vm --test functions
- INSTA_UPDATE=always cargo test -p baml_tests test_06_codegen
- cargo test -p baml_tests test_06_codegen

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Release Notes

* **Refactor**
* Updated function dispatch mechanism to use direct function name
references instead of index-based lookup.
* Enhanced function call resolution validation with earlier error
detection.
  * Streamlined direct-call handling in the compiler pipeline.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
baml-language: make '_' discard handling consistent in match lowering (#3144)

## Summary
- Stop materializing `_` as a local match binding in TIR and MIR so
discard behavior is consistent across switch and if-else lowering paths.
- Extend switch-path typed binding handling so typed discard patterns
like `_: Type` also skip local insertion.
- Add a VM regression test for typed discard patterns on the type-tag
switch path.

## Validation
- cargo test -p baml_compiler_tir
- cargo test -p baml_compiler_emit --test match_expr
- cargo test -p bex_vm --test match_expr

* **Bug Fixes**
* Fixed discard patterns (underscore `_`) in pattern matching to no
longer allocate unnecessary resources during compilation and type
inference.

* **Tests**
  * Added test coverage for typed discard patterns in match expressions.

commit.message:
Refactor call ABI: direct/indirect calls and frame-local setup (#3141)

## Summary
- change VM call ABI to split direct and indirect calls: CALL with
global callee index for statically-known callees, and CALL_INDIRECT for
runtime function values
- remove InitLocals instruction and move local-slot setup into frame
metadata via Function.real_local_count
- update emitter, pull semantics, and stack-carry simulation to match
the new call stack layouts and keep analysis/emission behavior aligned
- update test-friendly codegen representation for direct calls by name
and refresh compiler snapshots
- fix debug formatting so CALL metadata resolves compile-time globals to
readable function names

## Tests
- cargo test -p bex_vm --test functions
- cargo test -p baml_tests test_06_codegen
- cargo test -p baml_compiler_emit

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Release Notes

* **Refactor**
* Optimized bytecode generation for improved local variable
initialization and function call efficiency
* Updated compiler internals to emit cleaner, more direct function calls
  * Streamlined stack-handling logic for better code generation

* **Tests**
  * Updated test expectations to reflect compiler optimizations

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
bex_engine: add CancellationToken support across all layers (#3136)

## Summary

- **Phase 1 (Core):** Introduce `CancellationToken` (wrapping
`tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken`), add `EngineError::Cancelled`
variant, integrate biased `tokio::select!` in the VM event loop so
cancellation is checked before every await, and use `AbortHandle` to
kill spawned tasks on cancel.
- **Phase 2 (Sys Ops):** Wire `CancellationToken` through `SysOpContext`
so all sys_ops (sleep, HTTP fetch, etc.) respect cancellation. Add
`baml.sys.is_cancelled()` builtin for cooperative polling.
- **Phase 3 (Orchestrator):** Add cooperative `is_cancelled()` checks in
`llm.baml` at retry loops, primitive/fallback branches, and the
top-level `call_llm_function` entry point.
- **Phase 4 (Bindings):** Python `AbortController` class with
`abort()`/`aborted` API, WASM `callFunction(name, args, callId?)` +
`cancelCall(callId)` pattern, C FFI pass-through (already wired in Phase
1).
- **Tests:** 8 integration tests in `bex_engine/tests/cancellation.rs`
covering immediate cancel, sleep/HTTP interruption, selective
cancellation, sequential sleeps, idempotent cancel, cooperative
`is_cancelled()`, and normal completion.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test -p bex_engine --test cancellation` — all 8 tests pass
- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace` — clean
- [x] All existing tests pass (snapshot updates for `is_cancelled`
global index shift)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Execution now stops immediately when a cancellation is detected across
retries, fallbacks, and orchestration.
* Added a builtin to allow code to query whether the current call has
been cancelled.

* **Breaking Changes**
* Generated sys-op method signatures were adjusted—callers may need to
adapt to the new parameter ordering/format.

* **Chores**
  * Added a new workspace dependency to support these changes.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Enhanced jump threading + emit analysis soundness fixes (#3134)

## Summary

- **Enhanced jump threading**:
`build_redirect_targets_with_classifications()` threads through MIR
blocks that are "effectively empty" — all statements assign to
Dead/Virtual/CopyOf locals. This catches cases where expression-oriented
lowering generates `dest = null` in empty else branches and void loop
bodies.
- **Block-skipping in emit**: Redirect-source blocks are no longer
emitted, eliminating dead code from the bytecode output.
- **Single reclassification pass**: After enhanced threading,
classifications are compared against the new redirect map; if redirects
changed, a single reclassification pass ensures consistency.
- **3 soundness bug fixes**:
1. PhiLike/CallResultImmediate stack-order violation — stack-carried
values must be consumed in first-pull position
2. Cross-block virtual off-by-one in `has_side_effects_between` — was
missing side effects at the definition site
3. Multi-def virtual unsoundness — locals with multiple defs were
incorrectly virtualized; added projection-read guard for cross-block
safety

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test -p baml_compiler_emit` — all emit unit tests pass
(including 3 new soundness regression tests in `stack_soundness.rs` and
5 in `analysis_soundness.rs`)
- [x] `cargo test -p bex_vm` — VM execution tests pass
- [x] `cargo test -p baml_tests -- test_06_codegen` — codegen snapshots
updated (31 files, reduced jump counts)
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass (fmt, clippy)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Improved jump-threading and block-emission to remove redundant jumps,
skip empty blocks, and produce more consistent bytecode; made
local-materialization and virtualization rules more conservative to
preserve correct evaluation order and side-effect safety.
* **Tests**
* Updated many expectations to match new emission behavior and added
stack-discipline soundness tests covering operand materialization,
phi-like cases, and cross-block side-effect ordering.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
fixed let annotations (#3115)

**Summary**
This PR fixes diagnostics for annotated `let` assignments so type
mismatches include both:
- the existing primary span on the mismatched RHS expression, and
- a secondary info span pointing to the type annotation that introduced
the requirement.

It keeps the existing diagnostic code/message/phase behavior (`E0001`,
`Expected ..., found ...`, type phase), and only improves span context.

**Problem**
For `let` annotations like `let y: int = 10.0;`, the typechecker emitted
only one span (on `10.0`).
It should also show where the expected type came from (the `int`
annotation).

**What Changed**
- **Type-checking flow (`baml_compiler_tir`)**
- Added a scoped `check_expr_with_info_location(...)` path for passing
optional constraint-source location.
- Annotated `let` initializers now pass the annotation span as
`info_location`.
- Mismatch emission now carries that `info_location` through to
diagnostics.
- **Diagnostic conversion (`baml_compiler_diagnostics`)**
- `TypeMismatch.info_location` now maps to a **secondary annotation**
(`with_secondary`) instead of related-info metadata.
  - Secondary label text: `Type required here`.
- **Tests**
- Added unit coverage for `TypeMismatch` with `info_location` in
`to_diagnostic`.
- Updated `let_annotations.baml` expected diagnostics output to include
secondary spans.
- **Lint/compliance cleanup**
- Adjusted `info_location` passing to satisfy strict clippy
(`Option<&ErrorLocation>` + `.cloned()`), no behavior change.

**Before / After**
- **Before**: one span per mismatch (RHS only).
- **After**: same primary RHS span + secondary span on annotation token.
  - `let y : int = 10.0;` -> secondary on `int` (line 5, cols 11–13)
  - `let c: int = "hello";` -> secondary on `int` (line 6, cols 10–12)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Release Notes

* **Bug Fixes**
* Enhanced error messages for type mismatches to display secondary
annotations indicating where the expected type constraint originates,
providing clearer guidance for resolving type-related issues in your
code.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
