commit.message: Improve match arm analysis and diagnostic messages (#3104) Note - we now detect that `rock: Rock` is unreachable (because `Rock` isn't a subtype of `Pet`, no offense to rock lovers) - `_` in this example is also unreachable because `Pet` matching is exhausted by the two subtypes matches `Cat` and `Dog` - Exhaustiveness error messages are linked to their patterns, rather than the full arm ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Diagnostics now emit multiple entries per issue, including related-location hint diagnostics. * Match diagnostics report pattern locations more precisely. * Public API expanded to expose match-arm details for diagnostics. * **Bug Fixes** * Improved unreachable-pattern detection and more accurate pattern span tracking. * Better type-mismatch reporting tied to pattern locations. * **Tests** * Updated and added tests for exhaustiveness, reachability, diagnostic positioning, and match emission behavior.

commit.message:
baml_fmt: basically a formatter (#3111)

## Core formatter (crates/baml_fmt/, ~7,900 lines):

- `lib.rs` — Public API: `format(source, options) -> Result<String,
FormatterError>`. Refuses to format files with parse errors.
- `trivia_classifier.rs` — Single forward pass over the CST that tags
each comment and blank line as leading, trailing, or before-EOF. Keeps
the printer free of lookahead.
- `ast/` — Strong AST layer with `FromCST` trait. Converts the lossless
Rowan CST into typed structs that are easy for the printer to consume,
similar to `syn` for rust.
`printer.rs` — Output engine with `Printable`/`PrintMultiLine` traits
and `Shape`-based width tracking. Uses the "try single-line, fallback to
multi-line" strategy throughout.

## Key formatting behaviors:

- Line width default: 100 columns, indent: 4 spaces
- Greedy width-fitting: nodes try to fit on one line before splitting
- LLM function body field ordering normalized to client first, prompt
second
- Trivia attachment: trailing comments stay trailing, leading comments
re-indent to match context
- Raw/block strings: content preserved, baseline indentation adjusted
(currently we emit as-is)
- TODO: Formatting expressions in attribute args (changes needed
upstream in the parser, currently we emit as-is)
- TODO: Formatting attributes inside of types (changes needed upstream
in the parser, currently we emit as-is)
- Chained expressions (`a.b.c().d`) flatten and break at field access
points
- Binary operator chains group by precedence family
- Trailing commas added in multi-line lists

## Parser fixes (prerequisite changes):

- `>>` token splitting when expecting `>` (e.g. for `Map<string,
Map<string, int>>`)
- Improved > handling in the parser
- Comment parsing at file end

## Test infrastructure (crates/baml_tests/):

- Idempotency verification: `format(format(x)) == format(x)` for all
valid files
- ~70 snapshot files covering all major language features

```
┌──────────────────────────────────┐
│       Source text (.baml)        │
└────────────────┬─────────────────┘
                 │
        lex + parse (external crates)
                 │
                 ▼
┌────────────────┴─────────────────┐
│    Rowan CST (lossless tree)     │
└───────┬─────────────────┬────────┘
        │                 │
        ▼                 ▼
 Phase 1:            Phase 2:
 classify_trivia()   SourceFile::from_cst()
 Tags each comment   CST → Strong AST via
 as leading,         the FromCST trait.
 trailing, or        Validates structure &
 before-EOF.         extracts typed fields.
        │                 │
        └────────┬────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
┌────────────────┴─────────────────┐
│  Printer::print()                │
│  Recursive Printable walk.       │
│  try single-line → multi-line.   │
└────────────────┬─────────────────┘
┌────────────────┴─────────────────┐
│      Formatted source text       │
└──────────────────────────────────┘
```

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Full BAML formatter with configurable line width/indent, CLI
integration, and per-file formatter tests.

* **Refactors**
* Comprehensive AST/printer/trivia framework improving multi-line
layouts and comment placement.
* Parser improvements for generics/type-arguments and expanded keyword
recognition.

* **Tests**
  * Added idempotency and snapshot tests for formatting.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Adjusted diagnostic positioning for an extra '>' case.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
LLM orchestration port (#3121)

## Summary

Moves LLM orchestration (fallback, round-robin, retry) from Rust into
BAML code running on the VM.

### Orchestration in BAML

- `OrchestrationStep` class (`primitive_client`, `delay_ms`) — used by
`build_plan` for plan-only queries
- `ExecutionResult` / `ExecutionContext` classes — used by the lazy
recursive executor
- `build_plan(c) -> OrchestrationStep[]` — recursively expands Client
tree into a flat step list (for plan introspection)
- `build_plan_with_state(c, state) -> PlanExpansion` — side-effect-free
plan expansion with planner-local RR state
- `execute_client(c, ctx, delay) -> ExecutionResult` — lazy recursive
executor with retry semantics; RR counters advance only at execution
time
- `execute_client_once(c, ctx, delay) -> ExecutionResult` — single
attempt: Primitive sends HTTP, Fallback iterates children, RoundRobin
picks one child
- `call_llm_function` — creates ExecutionContext, delegates to
`execute_client`, panics on total failure
- `resolve_primitive`, `render_prompt`, `build_request` — shared helpers
for prompt rendering and HTTP request building

### Side-effect-free plan expansion

- `PlannerState` class tracks per-client RR selection counts locally
within a single planning run
- `planner_next_rr_offset` returns the current offset + updated state
(pure function, no atomics)
- `build_attempt_with_state` / `build_plan_with_state` thread planner
state through expansion
- `build_plan` / `build_attempt` are thin wrappers that start with empty
state
- `round_robin_peek` sys-op added for read-only access to runtime RR
counter (no increment)
- Regression test `plan_round_robin_has_no_runtime_side_effects`
verifies `build_plan` doesn't advance counters

### Orchestration parity with legacy

- **RR + retry rotation**: Round-robin rotates across retry attempts
(each retry picks a fresh child)
- **RR counter timing**: Counters advance at execution time only, not
during planning — nested RR inside fallback branches won't advance for
branches never reached
- **Last retry attempt delay**: Last local retry attempt carries no
local delay (falls back to inherited delay from outer wrappers)
- **Finish-reason filtering**: Enforced in parse path via existing
client options; raw provider finish reason preserved for correct
allow/deny matching
- **Round-robin start index**: `options { start ... }` parsed in HIR,
plumbed through emit → engine metadata for counter initialization
- **TODO(parity)**: Cancellation not yet wired (needs runtime-level
cancellation state)

### Builtin infrastructure

- Builtin enum support in `with_builtins!` macro (enum variants as
`EnumType.Variant`)
- `Client`, `ClientType`, `RetryPolicy` builtin types with all fields
- HIR retry policy parsing from `retry_policy` blocks (both flat and
sub-block syntax)
- Composite client metadata wiring through `SysOpContext` (sub_clients,
retry_policy, client_type)
- `resolve_client` sys_op — resolves a client name to a `FunctionRef`
pointing to `{name}.resolve`
- `baml.sys.sleep` and `baml.sys.panic` sys_ops
- `baml.math.trunc()` native function for float→int conversion
- `round_robin_next` with `AtomicUsize` counters per client in
`SysOpContext`
- `round_robin_peek` — read-only counter access for side-effect-free
planning
- `http.send` network error resilience — returns synthetic response with
`status_code=0`; `text()` now returns error details instead of failing

### FQN unification

- All builtin types use FQN consistently (e.g.
`baml.llm.OrchestrationStep`) — no more PRELUDE, no more dual-keying
- `ClassNamesSet` and `EnumNamesSet` changed from `HashSet<Name>` to
`HashMap<Name, QualifiedName>` — eliminates dot-detection heuristic for
namespace resolution
- `resolve_named_type()` and `resolve_name()` use map lookup instead of
string manipulation
- HIR `body.rs` handles `PATH_EXPR` nodes for dotted-path object
constructors (e.g. `baml.llm.OrchestrationStep { ... }`)
- TIR enum registration uses `enum_qualified_name` for consistency with
class registration

### HIR path resolution + LSP autocomplete

Moved multi-segment path resolution from TIR (trial-and-error) to HIR
(scope tree). Previously, TIR resolved paths like
`baml.llm.render_prompt` or `Status.Active` by trying builtin lookup →
function lookup → enum variant lookup in sequence with string
manipulation. Now HIR owns name resolution via a scope tree that mirrors
how compilers like rust-analyzer resolve names.

**Scope tree architecture (`path_resolve.rs`)**:
- Static builtin tree (computed once via `OnceLock`): Rust-defined
functions and enums under `baml.*`, `env.*`, etc.
- Dynamic symbol table (Salsa-cached per project): BAML-defined
functions and enums, looked up via O(1) hash lookups with properly
constructed `QualifiedName`s
- Per-enum Salsa query (`enum_variant_names`) — modifying one enum
doesn't invalidate resolution of unrelated paths

**TIR simplification**: The ~90-line trial-and-error chain in TIR's
`infer_expr` for multi-segment paths is replaced with a single
`resolve_path()` call + match on `PathResolution`. Variable + field
access chains remain as fallback for unresolved paths.

**LSP autocomplete for namespaces**: `scope_children()` enumerates all
items at a given scope path, enabling completions for `baml.`,
`baml.llm.`, `Status.`, etc. Previously typing `baml.llm.` produced no
completions. Wired into `complete_field_access` in the LSP completion
provider.

**New tracked Salsa queries**:
- `enum_qualified_name(db, enum_loc)` — qualified name for enums
(mirrors `class_qualified_name`)
- `enum_variant_names(db, enum_loc)` — per-enum variant set for
incremental resolution

### Compile-time validation improvements

- **EmptyStrategy (E0090)**: Composite clients (fallback/round-robin)
with no sub-clients in `strategy` now get a compile-time error instead
of a runtime crash
- **UnknownRetryPolicy (E0091)**: Cross-file validation — clients
referencing a non-existent retry policy name get a compile error with
span pointing to the reference
- **Strict retry policy parsing**: Invalid values for retry policy
fields (multiplier, delay, etc.) now produce compile errors instead of
silently defaulting

### Cleanup

- Removed `HIDDEN_LLM_BUILTINS` — these 6 functions were already in
`builtins()` from the `with_builtins!` macro, so they were being
double-registered with the first copy unreachable
- Dead code removal: `get_client_function` sys_op, `resolve_fn_name`
field, unused `builtin_enums` struct field, unused `accessor_supported`
function
- `retry_policy` duplicate detection now uses `item_name_key()` for
namespace-aware keying, consistent with all other item types
- Round-robin `unwrap_or_default()` replaced with explicit error for
unknown clients
- Replaced `unreachable!()` panics in codegen accessors with proper
`AccessError` returns for complex field types
- Clippy cleanup across `baml_builtins_macros`, `sys_types`,
`bex_engine`, `sys_native`, `bex_heap`

### Bug fixes

*Compiler pipeline:*
- **`let x = match (...)` expressions**: Match expressions weren't
supported as rvalues — added `MATCH_EXPR` handling in the expression
lowering path
- **Match on builtin enums**: Emit phase didn't generate `enum_variants`
for builtin enums like `ClientType`, so discriminant checks failed at
runtime
- **`retry_policy` keyword parsing**: `ConfigItem::key()` didn't handle
the `KW_RETRY_POLICY` syntax token
- **Flat retry policy syntax**: HIR `lower_retry_policy` only extracted
delay params from a `strategy` sub-block; added support for flat syntax
- **Field index mismatch for builtin classes**: `class_field_types`
keyed by FQN but `Ty::Class` used short name, causing field lookups to
fail
- **Exhaustiveness checker FQN mismatch**: Pattern enum_name used FQN
but expanded type values used short name — fixed by keying
`enum_variants()` by FQN and using `display_name()` consistently
- **Anonymous class panic**: HIR object literal parser only found direct
WORD token children, but dotted paths wrap tokens in a `PATH_EXPR` node
- **Empty segments panic**: `resolve_path` now guards against empty
segment slice
- **Assert literal fallback**: `lower_assert_stmt` now handles
`STRING_LITERAL`, `RAW_STRING_LITERAL`, and `FLOAT_LITERAL` tokens
- **Namespace-qualified method resolution**: Class method lookup and
self-type now use FQN for builtin-file classes

*Codegen/emit:*
- **`CallResultImmediate` breaking method calls**: Call results left on
stack got buried when used as arguments to subsequent
`Call`/`DispatchFuture` terminators
- **`CallResultImmediate` breaking class constructors**: `AllocInstance`
pushes onto the stack before field values are consumed, burying the call
result

*Runtime/BAML:*
- **Exponential backoff off-by-one**: Multiplication happened at end of
every loop iteration including attempt 0, producing wrong delays
- **`assert false` compilation**: Fixed `lower_assert_stmt` to handle
raw WORD tokens that aren't wrapped in expression nodes
- **Cycle detection**: `extract_class_deps` now uses
`fqn.display_name()` to match qualified keys in `class_field_types`

### Known issues

- **Type narrowing after early return** — BAML doesn't narrow
`RetryPolicy?` after `if (retry == null) { return inner; }`. Worked
around with `match` expression.

### Key files

- `baml_builtins/baml/llm.baml` — orchestration code (build_plan,
build_plan_with_state, execute_client, execute_client_once,
call_llm_function, resolve_primitive, render_prompt, build_request,
PlannerState)
- `baml_builtins/src/lib.rs` — `resolve_client`, `panic`,
`round_robin_peek` sys_ops, `OrchestrationStep`, `mod math { fn trunc
}`, removed `get_client_function`
- `baml_compiler_hir/src/path_resolve.rs` — scope tree for HIR-level
path resolution + LSP autocomplete (`resolve_path`, `scope_children`)
- `baml_compiler_hir/src/lib.rs` — `enum_qualified_name`,
`enum_variant_names`, `validate_retry_policy_refs`, method self-type FQN
fix
- `baml_compiler_hir/src/client.rs` — `EmptyStrategy` validation,
`round_robin_start` extraction, `retry_policy_span` tracking
- `baml_compiler_hir/src/symbol_table.rs` — functions/enums use
`function_qualified_name`/`enum_qualified_name` for proper
namespace-aware keying
- `baml_compiler_tir/` — multi-segment path resolution replaced with
`resolve_path()` call, `HashMap<Name, QualifiedName>` for class/enum
names, FQN for method resolution
- `baml_compiler_emit/` — removed `HIDDEN_LLM_BUILTINS`
double-registration, `CallResultImmediate` safety checks, round-robin
start plumbing, strict retry policy parsing
- `baml_compiler_vir/src/lower.rs` — `InvalidRetryPolicyValue` error
variant for strict retry parse failures
- `baml_lsp_actions/src/completion/providers.rs` —
`complete_field_access` uses `scope_children` for namespace autocomplete
- `sys_llm/src/lib.rs` — finish-reason filtering in parse path
- `sys_llm/src/parse_response/` — raw finish reason preservation
(OpenAI, Anthropic)
- `sys_types/src/lib.rs` — `resolve_client`, `round_robin_next`,
`round_robin_peek`, `round_robin_start` in metadata
- `sys_native/` — `sleep`, `panic`, synthetic error responses with
readable `text()`
- `bex_engine/tests/orchestration.rs` — 18 integration tests including
RR+retry rotation and side-effect-free planning

## Test plan

- [x] All 18 orchestration integration tests passing
- [x] All 375 baml_tests passing
- [x] All 13 llm_render tests passing
- [x] All 79 sys_llm tests passing
- [x] Retry policy validation test passing
- [x] Cargo clippy clean
- [x] Snapshots updated
- [x] `http.send` on unreachable URL returns synthetic response with
`status_code=0`
- [x] `text()` on error response returns error details
- [x] LSP namespace completion for `baml.`, `baml.llm.`, etc.
- [x] `build_plan` does not advance runtime RR counters
- [ ] End-to-end: BAML project with retry + fallback client compiles and
orchestrates correctly

commit.message:
Add emit analysis soundness regression tests (#3127)

## Summary
- add `analysis_soundness.rs` to `baml_compiler_emit` tests
- cover cross-block virtual inlining soundness, mutable-parameter copy
propagation, transitive dependency soundness, and side-effect
preservation
- assert emitted instruction shape directly to guard analysis/codegen
regressions

## Validation
- `cargo test -p baml_compiler_emit --test analysis_soundness`
- `cargo test -p baml_compiler_emit`


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Tests**
* Added comprehensive soundness tests for bytecode generation
validation. Tests ensure proper compilation of variable operations,
control flow handling, parameter mutations, and side effect preservation
across various scenarios. Coverage includes cross-block mutations,
transitive parameter modifications, mutable parameter copying, and
complex compilation patterns where multiple definitions interact.

commit.message:
baml-language: implement baml_types::Ty::Opaque (#3125)

Consolidating PromptAst, Resource, and Type into Opaque means that when
we add new builtin types in the future, we have to make fewer code
changes.

Slack discussion:
https://gloo-global.slack.com/archives/C0958DV7YPL/p1771361402317269?thread_ts=1770925371.154739&cid=C0958DV7YPL

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Unified runtime representation for opaque types, simplifying type
handling and method resolution.

* **New Features**
* Added public constructors and accessors for creating and inspecting
opaque runtime types.

* **Tests**
* Expanded tests for opaque-type matching, substitution, rendering,
display, and unsupported-type errors.

* **Chores**
* Updated codebase to use the new opaque-type APIs; error messages and
rendering now report opaque type names.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Serde implementations on generated types for Rust (#3118)

Added serde implementations to generated types for the Rust SDK, based
on the JSON layout used in other SDKs:
- Classes are laid out normal objects, with dynamic fields at the same
level (using `#[serde(flatten)]`
- Enums are represented as strings, including dynamic variants (using
`#[serde(untagged)]` on the dynamic variant)
- Unions are represented un-tagged (using `#[serde(untagged)]` on the
rust enum). This means that serialization+deserialization may be lossy,
as the implementation will select the first valid variant for
deserialization.

Some types cause errors if you try to deserialize:
- Streaming types cannot be deserialized, similar to how they do not
have `BamlEncode` implemented.
- `Types` and `StreamTypes`, the two top-level enumerations of all
generated types, cannot be deserialized due to too much ambiguity as to
which variant to make: it will almost always be incorrect.
- `DynamicClass`, `DynamicEnum`, and `DynamicUnion` which are the
fallback "fully dynamic" versions of these structures. Due to not
knowing the associated `name`, we cannot deserialize to these types.
Since these only seem to be used in `BamlValue`, and (as noted later) we
never deserialize `BamlValue` to a variant containing any of these
types, this should not be an issue unless a user directly tries to use
one of these types.
- If you try to _directly_ deserialize media types they will error
(since the `Deserialize` implementations in the `baml` crate cannot
access the runtime directly). However, any time they appear as a field
or union variant, we override the field/variant's deserializer to
correctly deserialize the media (note: media deserialization could cause
funny behavior if we are deserializing a union with multiple media
types, where an image could be deserialized as an audio file, for
example, due to lack of union disambiguation. Directly deserializing the
types would not fix this).

While `BamlValue` does have `Deserialize` implemented, it will map all
types into scalars, arrays, and maps. This means that dynamic types will
often be typed incorrectly. However, since we do not have any type
information on the dynamic types during deserialization, this is the
best we can do here.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Generated Rust types (classes, enums, unions, value containers,
streams) now support broad JSON serialization and many deserialization
paths.
* Media objects (image, audio, PDF, video) accept URL or Base64 payloads
and deserialize via runtime-backed handlers.
* Dynamic fields are serialized/deserialized inline (flattened);
streaming types gain serialization with explicit guards for ambiguous
deserialization.

* **Tests**
* Added extensive integration tests covering serde (de)serialization and
edge cases.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
fix: MIR analysis soundness bugs + emit simplifications (#3126)

## Summary

Fixes three miscompilation bugs in the MIR analysis pass and simplifies
the emit infrastructure with several targeted refactors. All changes are
in `analysis.rs`, `emit.rs`, `builder.rs`, and VM regression tests.

### Bug fixes (3 correctness issues)

- **Cross-block Virtual inlining unsoundness** — `can_be_virtual()`
would re-evaluate rvalues at use sites without checking whether
dependencies (including transitive ones and parameters with implicit
entry defs) could be modified on intermediate paths. Three related
fixes:
- Multi-def check: refuse to virtualize if any dependency has multiple
definitions
- Parameter awareness: parameters have an implicit entry def not in
`all_defs`, so any explicit reassignment means multiple defs
- Transitive tracking: use `collect_transitive_reads` instead of direct
`walk_rvalue_locals` to catch `let t = p; let x = t; p = 2; x` chains
- **CopyOf propagation for mutable parameters** — `get_copy_source()`
assumed parameters are never reassigned, but BAML allows `x = 3` where
`x` is a param. Added check that source parameter has no entries in
`all_defs`.
- **MIR construction invariant assertions** — Added `debug_assert!`
guards at MIR builder boundaries enforcing that
Call/Await/DispatchFuture destinations are always `Place::Local`.

### Refactors

- **`StatementRef` enum** — Replaced `TERMINATOR_IDX = usize::MAX`
sentinel with `enum StatementRef { Statement(usize), Terminator }` for
type-safe, exhaustiveness-checked references. All comparison sites now
use pattern matching.
- **Unified MIR tree walkers** — Consolidated 6 duplicate
rvalue/operand/place walker functions into 3 generic
`walk_{rvalue,operand,place}_locals` functions + 3 thin wrappers. Single
source of truth for tree traversal.
- **Merged def-use collection** — Combined `collect_def_use()` and
`collect_all_definitions()` into one pass by adding `all_defs:
Vec<(BlockId, StatementRef)>` to `LocalDefUse`. Eliminated a redundant
full-MIR walk.
- **Deduplicated `patch_jumps`** — `patch_jumps()` now delegates to
`patch_jump_to()` instead of duplicating the match logic.
- **Dead code removal** — Removed always-true `is_inlinable_rvalue()`,
unused `LocalDefUse::local` field, and made `emit_store_place`
Field/Index arms `unreachable!()`.
- **Struct cleanup** — Removed `dominators`/`predecessors` from
`AnalysisResult` (computation unchanged, just not stored in return value
since callers never read them). Simplified `entry().or_insert_with()`
boilerplate to `get_mut().unwrap()`.

## Test plan

- [x] 5 regression tests added for the 3 soundness bugs:
  - `virtual_cross_block_soundness` — cross-block variable modification
- `virtual_cross_block_param_mutation_soundness` — parameter mutation on
intermediate path
- `virtual_cross_block_transitive_param_mutation_soundness` — transitive
`t = p; x = t` chain
  - `copy_of_mutable_param_soundness` — CopyOf with reassigned param
- `virtual_multiple_defs_preserve_side_effects` — multiple defs must not
skip side effects
- [x] All existing tests pass: 375 baml_tests, 146 emit tests, 270+ VM
tests
- [x] `cargo fmt` and `cargo clippy` clean

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Improved internal compiler analysis and code generation structure for
enhanced maintainability and code organization.

* **Tests**
* Extended test coverage for variable assignment handling and virtual
inlining across different control flow scenarios.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Add InitLocals instruction for bulk local initialization (#3122)

## Summary
- Adds a new `InitLocals(n)` bytecode instruction that pushes `n` null
values onto the stack in one shot, replacing N consecutive
`LoadConst(null)` instructions emitted at the start of every function
for local variable pre-allocation.
- Uses `Vec::resize` for the VM handler instead of per-element push,
avoiding N dispatch cycles through the main match loop.
- Reduces bytecode size across all 33 codegen snapshots (e.g., 7
instructions → 1 for the common `baml.llm.call_llm_function` prologue).

## Test plan
- [x] All 139 `baml_compiler_emit` tests pass
- [x] All 375 `baml_tests` pass (33 codegen snapshots auto-updated)
- [x] All 24 `bex_vm` tests pass
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` clean
- [x] `cargo clippy` clean on affected crates

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactor**
* Consolidated local variable pre-allocation into a single bytecode
initialization step instead of multiple per-slot null pushes.
* **New Features**
* VM and bytecode now recognize an explicit local-initialization
instruction; debugger output and coloring updated accordingly.
* **Tests**
* Updated emitted-code expectations and test harness to reflect the
consolidated local initialization.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Add tracing capability (#3107)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> Touches core execution (`call_function`/VM `exec`) and heap object
model by adding tracing notifications, a new object type, and global
event buffering, which can affect runtime behavior and performance
across all calls.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds end-to-end runtime tracing by introducing a new `bex_events`
crate (span IDs/contexts, in-memory event store with JSONL flush, and
`Collector`/`FunctionLog` views) and wiring it into `bex_engine`.
> 
> Extends `BexEngine::call_function` to accept an optional
`HostSpanContext` plus attached collectors, emits root + nested traced
span start/end events, and deep-copies VM values for event payloads.
> 
> Updates the VM/bytecode pipeline to support tracing via a per-function
`trace` flag (enabled for LLM functions) and new
`VmExecState::SpanNotify` enter/exit notifications; adds a new heap
object/ADT type `Collector` to pass collector handles through the
runtime. Tests are updated and new tracing tests are added;
workspace/build config is adjusted (new deps, new `bridge_python` crate,
size-gate baselines, and ignore `debug_events*`).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
bf1143cdd0c7a1d69a483611d79f441516395433. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Python package for running BAML (sync/async) with Collector and
FunctionLog types; host-span manager and context helpers.
* Runtime tracing with span start/end events, JSONL export, per-span
collectors, and an in-memory event store.

* **Improvements**
* Engine call API accepts host span context and collectors to propagate
tracing and collect logs.

* **Tests**
* Large end-to-end and unit test suites for tracing, collectors, and
Python bridge.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add int/float coercion for binary and comparison ops (#3120)

## Summary
- The type checker already allows mixed int/float operations (e.g. `int
* float → float`), but the VM was missing match arms for these cases,
causing runtime errors
- Adds int-to-f64 promotion for `BinOp` (+, -, *, /, %) and `CmpOp` (==,
!=, <, <=, >, >=) with mixed int/float operands
- Bitwise ops on mixed int/float correctly error (same as float/float)

## Test plan
- [x] All 260 existing bex_vm tests pass
- [x] Clippy clean with `-D warnings`

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Integer and floating-point values can now be used together in
arithmetic operations, with automatic promotion to floating-point
calculations.
* Integer and floating-point values can now be directly compared, with
automatic type promotion for consistent results.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Propagate @stream.done to union variants for atomic list-item streaming (#3119)

## Summary
- Fixes `(T @stream.done)[]` not filtering incomplete list items when
`T` is a union type
- Propagates the `done` flag from Union to its variants in the streaming
type converter, mirroring the existing behavior for List and Map inner
types
- Adds 4 regression tests covering incomplete, all-incomplete,
all-complete, and `@@stream.done` workaround scenarios

## Bug
When streaming a function returning `(OutputItem @stream.done)[]` where
`OutputItem = ToolCall | ExampleMessage`, incomplete list items appeared
with placeholder/null fields and updated later, instead of only
appearing once fully complete.

**Root cause:** The streaming type converter (`streaming.rs`) propagated
`done` from List → inner items and Map → keys/values, but did **not**
propagate `done` from Union → variants. Without this, variant classes
stayed in `Streaming` mode (optional fields), allowing incomplete JSON
objects to be coerced successfully during streaming.

Bug report:
https://discord.com/channels/1119368998161752075/1467029752606101660/1467190506990600242

## Test plan
- [x] Added `test_union_stream_done_list_incomplete_item` — incomplete
2nd item filtered out
- [x] Added `test_union_stream_done_list_all_incomplete` — all
incomplete → empty list
- [x] Added `test_union_stream_done_list_all_complete` — all complete →
all shown

author Antonio Sarosi <sarosiantonio@gmail.com> 1771246010 +0100
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1771246010 +0000

Propagate @stream.done to union variants for atomic list-item streaming (#3119)

## Summary
- Fixes `(T @stream.done)[]` not filtering incomplete list items when
`T` is a union type
- Propagates the `done` flag from Union to its variants in the streaming
type converter, mirroring the existing behavior for List and Map inner
types
- Adds 4 regression tests covering incomplete, all-incomplete,
all-complete, and `@@stream.done` workaround scenarios

## Bug
When streaming a function returning `(OutputItem @stream.done)[]` where
`OutputItem = ToolCall | ExampleMessage`, incomplete list items appeared
with placeholder/null fields and updated later, instead of only
appearing once fully complete.

**Root cause:** The streaming type converter (`streaming.rs`) propagated
`done` from List → inner items and Map → keys/values, but did **not**
propagate `done` from Union → variants. Without this, variant classes
stayed in `Streaming` mode (optional fields), allowing incomplete JSON
objects to be coerced successfully during streaming.

Bug report:
https://discord.com/channels/1119368998161752075/1467029752606101660/1467190506990600242

## Test plan
- [x] Added `test_union_stream_done_list_incomplete_item` — incomplete
2nd item filtered out
- [x] Added `test_union_stream_done_list_all_incomplete` — all
incomplete → empty list
- [x] Added `test_union_stream_done_list_all_complete` — all complete →
all shown
- [x] Added `test_union_block_stream_done_list_incomplete` —
`@@stream.done` workaround still works
- [x] All 471 existing jsonish tests pass

-->

commit.message:
Add cargo-size-gate for binary size gating in CI (#3110)

## Summary

- Adds `cargo-size-gate`, a new workspace binary crate that builds,
measures, and gates binary sizes for `bridge_cffi` (native cdylib) and
`bridge_wasm` (WASM) against committed TOML baselines
- Enforces absolute gzip size limits and delta percentage policies per
artifact, with actionable error output including exact TOML diffs for
updating baselines from CI
- Adds CI size-gate checks for all 4 targets (linux, macos, windows,
wasm) with a unified PR comment showing per-platform results with status
emojis and collapsible details

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check -p cargo-size-gate` compiles
- [x] `cargo clippy -p cargo-size-gate` passes
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass (fmt, stow, clippy, yaml, toml)
- [x] `cargo run -p cargo-size-gate -- size-gate record` writes baseline
files
- [x] `cargo run -p cargo-size-gate -- size-gate check` passes against
recorded baselines
- [x] `cargo run -p cargo-size-gate -- size-gate check --format md`
produces valid markdown
- [x] Policy violation produces actionable error with TOML diff
- [x] Missing baseline produces actionable error with TOML snippet
- [ ] CI workflow runs size-gate on all 4 platforms
- [ ] PR comment is posted with unified report

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added a cargo-based size-gate tool with record/check/diff/aggregate
commands, producing table/Markdown/JSON outputs and aggregated CI
reports.
* CI now runs cross-platform size gates (Linux/macOS/Windows/WASM),
uploads per-platform reports, and publishes a unified size summary.

* **Chores**
* Added baseline/config support and precomputed baseline manifests for
consistent size checks.
* Made HTTP support opt-in via a feature flag and adjusted
dependency/feature wiring.
  * Added workspace tasks to run and record size gates.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
VM cleanup: remove dead code, refactor watch graph, fix GC tracing (#3108)

## Summary

- Remove `PopReplace` instruction from bytecode enum, VM handler, debug
display, and test helpers. The MIR compiler never emits it — block
expressions assign the trailing value directly into the destination
`Place`, so there's no stack-based scope cleanup needed.

- Remove implicit watch cleanup from `Pop` and `Return` handlers. With
the MIR's explicit `Unwatch` instruction, these were dead code.
`Unwatch` is now the single canonical site for watch variable cleanup.

- Unify `StoreVar`'s watch edge logic with the existing
`update_watched_node` helper, eliminating duplicated
unlink/link/deep-copy code.

- Replace function cloning in the exec loop with unsafe
`load_function()` returning `&'static Function`. Since all function
objects live in the compile-time heap (never GC'd), `'static` is sound.
This removes 11 redundant function re-clone sites, keeping reassignment
only at `Call(Bytecode)` and `Return` where the frame actually changes.
Includes a TODO documenting what needs to change when lambdas with
captures are added.

- Refactor `watch.rs` graph internals: merge the two-pass pattern
(`track_watch_dependencies` walking the heap to build edges, then
`link_edge` walking the graph to propagate reachability) into a single
BFS that does both simultaneously. Extract `mark_reachable` helper to
deduplicate the reachability propagation logic that was inlined in both
functions. Remove `link_edge` from `Watch`'s public API since it's no
longer called from the VM. Fix stale graph edge accumulation by pruning
`children`/`parents` entries when a node becomes unreachable from all
roots. Replace `HashSet` clone with insert-swap in `unlink_edge`.
Rewrite unit tests to use real `HeapPtr` from `Box` instead of fake
pointers, testing `track_watch_dependencies` directly.

- Fix GC not tracing `Watch` state. `RootState`'s
`last_assigned`/`last_notified` values are deep-copied off the stack, so
GC could collect them. Graph `NodeId`s contain `HeapPtr`s that go stale
after a copying GC. Added `collect_roots()` and `apply_forwarding()` to
`Watch`, wired into both GC sites in `bex_engine`.

- Change `instruction_ptr` from `isize` to `usize`. The instruction
pointer is a natural index into the instructions vec. Jump offsets
(`isize`) are now applied via `checked_add_signed` at the 3 jump sites.
This removes 6 clippy cast allows, the `NegativeInstructionPtr` error
variant, and the per-instruction negativity check from the hot loop.
Also re-enables the commented-out instruction tracing under a
`BEX_VM_DEBUG` env var (debug builds only).

## Test plan
- [x] All 295 `bex_vm` tests pass (including 5 watch unit tests + 18
watch integration tests)
- [x] All 375 `baml_tests` pass
- [x] All 75 `bex_engine` tests pass (including 5 GC tests)
- [x] `cargo clippy` clean

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Breaking Changes**
* Removed support for the POP_REPLACE instruction; it will no longer
appear in bytecode or test output.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Ensured watch state stays synchronized during garbage collection.
  * Improved error reporting for jump/offset overflow scenarios.

* **Internal Improvements**
* Refactored VM internals for safer instruction pointer handling and
streamlined function access (no behavioral change exposed).
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
feat(wasm): bind env_vars callback to BAML WASM runtime (#3103)

## Summary
- Add `SysOpsBuilder::with_env_instance` in baml_builtins_macros (same
pattern as HTTP)
- Add `WasmEnv` in bridge_wasm implementing `SysOpEnv` via JS callback
`(var: string) => string | undefined`
- `BamlWasmRuntime::create` now takes `env_vars_fn`; wire WasmEnv in
SysOps
- TypeScript: `WasmEnvVarsCallback` type and updated `create()`
signature in .d.ts and call sites (SplitPreview, bridge_wasm.test.ts)

## Usage
```ts
BamlWasmRuntime.create(rootPath, srcFilesJson, fetchFn, (varName) => process.env[varName]);
```

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* WASM runtime now supports environment-variable lookups via a
JavaScript callback.

* **Chores / API**
* Runtime construction now accepts a single callbacks/options object
(fetch and env) instead of a lone fetch function; public create
signature updated.

* **Tests**
* Test and usage sites updated to the new callbacks/options contract;
HMR test timeouts increased for stability.

commit.message:
Port env from native function to sys_op, remove env_vars from pipeline (#3102)

## Summary
- Convert `env.get` and `env.get_or_panic` from native VM functions to
sys_ops that call `std::env::var()` directly
- Remove `env_vars: HashMap<String, String>` from BexVm, BexEngine,
BexFactory, and CFFI bridge — env vars are now read at runtime instead
of cloned through the entire pipeline
- Add `ENV_ACCESS_EXPR` syntax node so the parser properly handles
`env.X` syntax (previously broken — lexer produced `TokenKind::Env` but
parser only checked `TokenKind::Word`)
- Desugar `env.X` → `env.get_or_panic("X")` during HIR lowering;
`env.get(...)` calls resolve normally through the env module
- Support `Option<T>` return types in sys_op codegen and
`AsBexExternalValue`
- Fix `module_from_path` for 2-segment paths like `env.get` (was
incorrectly extracting `"get"` as module name)

## Test plan

commit.message:
Add bex_factory crate, update bridge_cffi to use it (#3098)

## Summary
- Add `bex_factory` crate: a reusable facade wrapping the
compile-and-run pipeline (`BexFactory` struct with `new()`,
`call_function()`, `function_params()`)
- Update `bridge_cffi` to depend on `bex_factory` instead of directly on
`baml_compiler_emit`, `baml_project`, `bex_engine`,
`bex_external_types`, `baml_base`, and `sys_types`
- Fix pre-existing clippy warnings (`dead_code` in `baml_compiler_mir`,
`baml_compiler_tir`; unused import in `bex_heap`)

commit.message:
Compile LLM functions to bytecode calling call_llm_function (#3094)

## Summary

### Compile LLM functions to bytecode
- LLM functions (e.g. `Greet`) were emitted as `FunctionKind::SysOp`
with empty bytecode, causing a `TypeError` when called from other
bytecode functions via a regular `Call` instruction
- Fix: generate a synthetic `ExprBody` for each LLM function that calls
`baml.llm.call_llm_function(name, args_map)`, mirroring how client
`.resolve()` functions are synthesized
- Moved synthetic body generation from the emit phase to HIR lowering,
so LLM function bodies are visible in HIR/MIR snapshots and
type-checkable
- The orchestrator in `llm.baml` handles prompt rendering, HTTP, and
response parsing through properly dispatched sys_ops;
`FunctionMeta::Llm` metadata is preserved for engine-level lookups

### Qualified type paths & builtin diagnostics
- Parser now accepts dotted paths in type positions (e.g.,
`baml.http.Request`)
- CST, HIR, and TIR updated to extract, create, and resolve
multi-segment type paths
- `llm.baml` updated to use qualified names (`-> baml.llm.PromptAst`,
`-> baml.http.Request`) to avoid collisions with user-defined types
- Builtin files now included in `file_map` and `check()` diagnostics —
type errors in builtin function bodies are caught at compile time
- Builtin sources included in diagnostic rendering maps so errors render
with proper file:line:col

### Test infrastructure cleanup
- Replaced `TestDatabase` with `ProjectDatabase` everywhere
- Added diagnostic error checking to `compile_source()` — tests now fail
on type errors instead of silently compiling broken code
- Deleted `bex_program` crate — it only existed to build a `BexProgram`
struct, extract `.bytecode`, and throw everything else away. Engine
tests now call `compile_source()` directly.

### Proc macro DSL improvements
- Added `#[opaque]` attribute to the `with_builtins!` macro DSL for
types that need dedicated heap variants (replaces hardcoded `struct_name
== "PromptAst"` check)

### Key design decisions
- **`CompilerGenerated::LlmFunction` marker** (no fields) preserves
`ItemTree` early cutoff — body content changes don't invalidate
downstream queries
- **Separate `llm_function_meta` salsa query** extracts prompt/client
metadata from the CST without polluting the `ItemTree`
- **TIR dual-body approach**: `function_body` returns the synthetic
`Expr` body (for HIR/MIR/compilation), but TIR uses `FunctionBody::Llm`
(for Jinja validation + declared return type)
- **`call_llm_function` returns `unknown`** so synthetic bodies don't
produce return type mismatches (`Ty::BuiltinUnknown` — the `unknown`
keyword — accepts any type assigned to it but can't be used where a
concrete type is expected)
- **Malformed LLM functions** (parse errors in body) use
`FunctionBody::Missing` to skip body type-checking while still
type-checking the signature for LSP autocomplete

### Type checker fixes (surfaced by diagnostic checking)
- **Builtin class field visibility**: `lookup_class_field` used
`fqn.name` (e.g., `"Response"`) but `class_field_types` keyed by full
path (`"baml.http.Response"`). Fixed all 4 call sites to use
`display_name()`.
- **Map key subtyping**: empty map `{}` infers as `map<Ty::Unknown,
Ty::Unknown>` where `Ty::Unknown` is the internal error-recovery type
(distinct from `Ty::BuiltinUnknown`, which is the user-facing `unknown`
keyword). The invariance check `k1 == k2` failed when comparing
error-recovery `Unknown` against `string`. Fixed to treat
`Ty::Unknown`/`Ty::Error` keys as compatible with any key type.
- **`$watch` receiver type**: `$watch.options`/`$watch.notify` declared
receiver as inner type (`int`) instead of `WatchAccessor(int)`, causing
mismatch with the Call handler.
- **Union type operators**: Extended
`is_int_like`/`is_float_like`/`is_string_like`/`is_bool_like` to handle
`Ty::Union` recursively (e.g., `20 | 0` is int-like).
- **String comparisons**: Added `Lt`/`Le`/`Gt`/`Ge` support for string
types.

## Test plan
- [x] All 375 `baml_tests` pass (HIR, TIR, MIR, diagnostics, codegen
snapshots updated)
- [x] All 219 `baml_lsp_actions_tests` pass (watch_when snapshot
updated)
- [x] All 95 `baml_compiler_tir` tests pass
- [x] All `bex_engine` tests pass (concurrent, gc, http, llm_render,
typed_outputs)
- [x] All `bex_vm` tests pass (watch, match_expr, strings, etc.)
- [x] All `baml_compiler_emit` tests pass
- [x] Incremental tests verify `function_type_inference` re-executes on
prompt changes
- [x] Builtins included alongside source files for type-checking with
zero errors

commit.message:
Unify VIR schema and BexProgram types into   baml_type::defs (#3092)

Move canonical type definitions (ClassDef, EnumDef, FunctionDef, etc.)
  into baml_type::defs so VIR and BexProgram share the same structs,
  eliminating near-duplicate definitions and field name mismatches
  (field_type→ty, param_type→ty, body→body_kind). Also adds TypeAliasDef
  and type alias lowering in VIR schema.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added support for type aliases in schema definitions.
* Emitted programs now preserve and carry schema items directly
(schema-driven output).

* **Refactor**
* Introduced a single canonical set of schema definitions and exposed
them across components for a consistent public API.
* Consumer components now reuse shared schema types, simplifying schema
handling and reducing duplication.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Signed-off-by: hellovai <vaibhavtheory@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: coderabbitai[bot] <136622811+coderabbitai[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor baml_builtins_macros: split into modules, deduplicate, and improve readability (#3090)

## Summary

- **Split monolithic 2722-line `lib.rs`** into 7 focused modules:
`parse.rs`, `collect.rs`, `util.rs`, `codegen_builtins.rs`,
`codegen_native.rs`, `codegen_sys_ops.rs`, `codegen_accessors.rs`. The
new `lib.rs` is just 65 lines of thin entry points.
- **Replaced opaque tuples** in `NativeFnDef` (e.g., `receiver:
Option<(String, String, bool, bool)>`) with **named structs**
(`ReceiverInfo`, `ParamInfo`, `ReturnInfo`) — making all destructuring
sites self-documenting.
- **Extracted shared `CollectedBuiltins`** struct with a
`from_modules()` constructor, eliminating the duplicated 15-line
parse+collect block across all 4 proc macros.
- **Integrated `AccessorTypeDef` collection** into the shared walker
(`CollectContext`), removing the separate `collect_accessor_types` /
`collect_accessor_types_from_module` functions.
- **Unified utility functions**: removed `sys_op_ref_type_ident`
(callers now use `path_to_rust_ident` + HashMap lookup) and
`sys_op_receiver_name` (callers now use `to_snake_case`).
- **Removed unnecessary `Option` wrapper** from `BuiltinFieldDef.ty` (it
was always `Some`).

`parse.rs`, `collect.rs`, `util.rs`, `codegen_builtins.rs`,
`codegen_native.rs`, `codegen_sys_ops.rs`, `codegen_accessors.rs`. The
new `lib.rs` is just 65 lines of thin entry points.
- **Replaced opaque tuples** in `NativeFnDef` (e.g., `receiver:
Option<(String, String, bool, bool)>`) with **named structs**
(`ReceiverInfo`, `ParamInfo`, `ReturnInfo`) — making all destructuring
sites self-documenting.
- **Extracted shared `CollectedBuiltins`** struct with a
`from_modules()` constructor, eliminating the duplicated 15-line
parse+collect block across all 4 proc macros.
- **Integrated `AccessorTypeDef` collection** into the shared walker
(`CollectContext`), removing the separate `collect_accessor_types` /
`collect_accessor_types_from_module` functions.
- **Unified utility functions**: removed `sys_op_ref_type_ident`
(callers now use `path_to_rust_ident` + HashMap lookup) and
`sys_op_receiver_name` (callers now use `to_snake_case`).
- **Removed unnecessary `Option` wrapper** from `BuiltinFieldDef.ty` (it
was always `Some`).
- **Code-gen `builtin_types` module** in `bex_heap` from the
`with_builtins!` DSL (from prior work, included in this branch).

- Add linting and diagnostics for various errors within jinja templates
   - Name resolution errors
   - Type errors in template calls
   - Syntax errors in the template string

 - Improve spans for errors in type definitions within unions
 - Bump rust toolchain to 1.93.0 to match `engine`

commit.message:
Add build_request to CFFI, Go runtime/codegen, and Rust codegen (#3089)

## Summary

- Expose `build_request` and `build_request_stream` across the CFFI
boundary, Go runtime, Go code generator, Rust code generator, and the
Rust client SDK (`baml` + `baml-sys` crates), mirroring the existing
Python modular API
- CFFI layer: new `build_request_from_c` function with
`send_object_to_callback` for returning `HTTPRequest` object handles via
the async callback mechanism
- Go runtime: `BuildRequest` method on `BamlRuntime`, object-handle
callback decode path, C wrapper + symbol registration
- Go codegen: new `Request.FunctionName()` and
`StreamRequest.FunctionName()` namespaces via
`function.build_request.go.j2` / `function.build_request_stream.go.j2`
templates
- Rust codegen: `build_request` / `build_request_stream` methods added
to both sync and async client macros
- Rust SDK: `build_request_from_c` symbol in `baml-sys`, plus
`build_request`, `build_request_stream`, `build_request_async`,
`build_request_stream_async` on `BamlRuntime` with `InvocationResponse`
-> `BamlObjectHandle` -> `HTTPRequest` decoding
- Evaluate tests added for both Go and Rust (sample test data) covering
`build_request` and `build_request_stream` for sync and async

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test --lib -p baml_cffi` passes (1 test)
- [x] `cargo test --lib -p generators-go -- --skip evaluate` passes (137
tests)
- [x] `cargo test --lib -p generators-rust -- --skip evaluate` passes
(137 tests)
- [x] `cargo test --lib -p generators-rust -- evaluate` passes (all 18
evaluate tests including generated code compilation)
- [x] Go evaluate tests fail only due to pre-existing library version
mismatch (0.218.1 vs 0.217.0), unrelated to this change
- [x] New build_request tests in
`generators/data/sample/go/main_test.go` and
`generators/data/sample/rust/main.rs` compile successfully
- [ ] Run with `RUN_GENERATOR_TESTS=1` to execute build_request tests
end-to-end against a live LLM


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Build HTTP requests without executing them (sync & async), including
streaming variants; available via Go/Rust clients and FFI. Runtime and
client APIs now return HTTP request handles for inspection or reuse.

* **Tests**
* Added tests validating constructed requests: URL scheme, POST method,
non-empty headers, and JSON-parseable bodies for standard and streaming
flows.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Ergonomic sys_op traits with generic SysOpOutput<T> and auto-generated glue (#3088)

## Summary

- **Per-module sys_op traits** (`SysOpFs`, `SysOpSys`, `SysOpNet`,
`SysOpHttp`, `SysOpLlm`) auto-generated by `generate_sys_op_traits` proc
macro from DSL `#[sys_op]` annotations — eliminates manual function
signatures, argument extraction boilerplate, and error wrapping
- **Generic `SysOpOutput<T>`** enum where `T` defaults to
`BexExternalValue` — trait methods return typed values (e.g.
`SysOpOutput<FsFile>`, `SysOpOutput<String>`, `SysOpOutput<()>`) and the
generated glue converts to `SysOpResult` via `Into<BexExternalValue>`
automatically
- **`SysOps::from_impl<T>()`** constructor wires per-module trait impls
into the function pointer table, replacing manual field-by-field
assignment
- Added `From` impls for all owned builtin types (`FsFile`, `NetSocket`,
`HttpRequest`, `HttpResponse`, `PrimitiveClient`), `()` (→ Null), and
`PromptAst` → `BexExternalValue`
- `llm_ops::build_request` now returns `owned::HttpRequest` directly
instead of `BexExternalValue`
- Added `#[derive(Debug)]` to all owned builtin types

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo check` passes clean
- [x] All unit tests pass (`cargo test --lib -p sys_types -p sys_native
-p llm_ops -p bex_heap -p bex_external_types`)
- [x] All pre-commit hooks pass (fmt, clippy, stow)

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Engine-level system-op context with precomputed LLM metadata
* New owned-type LLM crate exposing prompt rendering, specialization,
request building, and response parsing
  * Native async system-ops provider for filesystem, network and HTTP

* **Refactoring**
  * Unified, generated system-op dispatch with per-module traits
* Renamed LLM client and consolidated resource-handle types across
crates

* **Chores**
  * Workspace dependency and manifest reorganization
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
Move Media and PromptAst into baml_builtins ADTs; add BexExternalAdt and FunctionRef; drop bex_value (#3085)

## Summary

Move the definitions of `MediaValue`/`MediaContent` and
`PromptAst`/`PromptAstSimple` into `baml_builtins` as the single source
of truth, introduce a dedicated external representation for these
builtin ADTs (`BexExternalAdt`), and remove the old `bex_value` module
in favor of `BexExternalValue` (and `BexExternalAdt`) as the FFI
boundary type.

## Changes

### baml_builtins
- **New `adt.rs`**: Defines `MediaValue`, `MediaContent`, `PromptAst`,
and `PromptAstSimple`.
- **MediaValue**: Holds `MediaContent` (Url, Base64, File) behind
`RwLock` + `UnsafeCell` with `read_content`/`write_content` and manual
`Sync` for async/`Arc` use.
- **PromptAst / PromptAstSimple**: Prompt AST (e.g. `Simple`, `Message {
role, content, metadata }`, `Vec` of nodes) with `merge_adjacent()` to
flatten and merge adjacent strings.
- Builtin BAML (`llm.baml`) orchestrators (`render_prompt`,
`build_request`, `call_llm_function`) now use and return these types.

### bex_vm_types
- `MediaValue` and `PromptAst` are now type aliases to
`Arc<baml_builtins::MediaValue>` and `Arc<baml_builtins::PromptAst>`;
heap `Object::Media` and `Object::PromptAst` use these.

### bex_external_types
- **Removed** `bex_value.rs`.
- **Extended** `bex_external_value.rs` with `BexExternalAdt` (Media,
PromptAst) and `BexExternalValue::Adt(BexExternalAdt)` so external/FFI
code can represent these builtins without heap pointers.
- **`BexExternalValue::FunctionRef { global_index }`**: Sys ops can
return a callable reference; `get_client_function` returns a
`FunctionRef` so the VM can call the client's resolve function (e.g.
`resolve_client_fn()` in `llm.baml`).

### Conversion, engine, heap
- **bex_engine conversion**: VM → external maps
`Object::Media`/`Object::PromptAst` to `BexExternalValue::Adt(...)`;
external → VM maps `Adt(Media)`/`Adt(PromptAst)` via
`alloc_media`/`alloc_prompt_ast`.
- **bex_heap accessor**: `BexValue::as_media_owned` and
`as_prompt_ast_owned` support
`BexExternalValue::Adt(BexExternalAdt::Media|PromptAst)`.
- **baml_type::typetag::PROMPT_AST**: New type tag for runtime
discrimination of `PromptAst`.

### LLM / Jinja / FFI
- **llm_jinja**: Converts `BexExternalValue::Adt(Media)` to Jinja
placeholders; rejects `Adt(PromptAst)` in templates; rendering produces
`baml_builtins::PromptAst`.
- **llm_ops**: `render_prompt` returns `PromptAst`;
build_request/specialize_prompt consume it.
- **bridge_cffi**: Encodes `Adt(Media)` as a string placeholder;
`Adt(PromptAst)` and `FunctionRef` treated as internal (e.g. null over
FFI).

### Cleanup and clippy
- **Dead code removed**: `GcProtectedHeap.heap` field,
`allocate_from_external`, test helper `msg_with_meta`.
- **SysOpFn** and all sys op implementations take `heap: &Arc<BexHeap>`;
llm/sys `execute_*` take `&Arc<BexHeap>`.
- Clippy fixes: unnecessary_wraps, needless_pass_by_value,
default_trait_access, manual_let_else,
match_wildcard_for_single_variants, redundant_closure, print_stderr,
unused vars.

### Tests
- **baml_tests vm**: Test `Object` includes `Media(kind)`;
`from_vm_object` bails on `PromptAst`.
- **bex_engine**: llm_render tests for direct render, chat roles, enums,
and e2e `render_prompt` flow; concurrent/gc/net tests updated.

### Snapshots
- Codegen snapshots updated for the new builtin/type surface.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* ADT-backed Media & Prompt types with media placeholders and deferred
resolution
  * Media-aware Jinja prompt rendering producing Arc-backed prompt ASTs
* Built-in GC-safe accessors and owned wrapper types for safer heap
interactions

* **Refactor**
* Unified external ADT representation and heap-backed, Arc-based data
flows for LLM paths
* System APIs and sysops migrated to guarded, owned argument/return
flows
  * Consolidated, structured error kinds and clearer error mapping

* **Bug Fixes**
* HTTP response bodies captured as text for more reliable parsing and
handling
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Signed-off-by: hellovai <vaibhavtheory@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: coderabbitai[bot] <136622811+coderabbitai[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rust CI cache problem (#3080) # Pull Request Template Thanks for taking the time to fill out this pull request! ## Issue Reference Please link to any related issues - [ ] This PR fixes/closes # (This PR addresses the Rust cache miss issue discussed in the conversation, referencing a similar issue: https://github.com/Swatinem/rust-cache/issues/9) ## Changes Please describe the changes proposed in this pull request This PR fixes a persistent Rust cache miss issue in CI workflows by: 1. **Reordering CI steps**: The `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2` action now runs *after* the Rust toolchain is installed via `rustup show`. This ensures the cache key is generated based on the project's `rust-toolchain.toml` version, not the GitHub runner's default Rust version. 2. **Adding `shared-key`**: Configured `shared-key` for `rust-cache` in various jobs (e.g., `linux-cargo`, `macos-cargo`, `windows-cargo`, `linux-wasm`, `linux-msrv`) to improve cache hit rates across similar CI runs. ## Testing Please describe how you tested these changes - [ ] Unit tests added/updated - [x] Manual testing performed (Internal diagnostics and verification of workflow file changes) - [ ] Tested in [environment] (Validation will occur via subsequent CI runs) ## Screenshots If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your changes [Add screenshots here...] ## PR Checklist Please ensure you've completed these items - [x] I have read and followed the contributing guidelines - [x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project - [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code - [x] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas - [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation - [x] My changes generate no new warnings ## Additional Notes Add any other context about the PR here ### Root Cause The `Swatinem/rust-cache@v2` action was running **BEFORE** the Rust toolchain was installed via `rustup show`. This caused the cache key to be generated using the pre-installed Rust version on GitHub runners (e.g., `1.93.0`) instead of your project's `rust-toolchain.toml` version (`1.89`). When GitHub updates their runner images with new Rust versions, ALL previous caches were invalidated. From the CI logs (before fix): ``` Rust Version: 1.93.0 x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu (254b59607d4417e9dffbc307138ae5c86280fe4c) ``` While `rust-toolchain.toml` specifies `channel = "1.89"`. ### Fix Applied 1. **Reordered steps** in all affected workflows to install the Rust toolchain BEFORE `rust-cache` runs: - `cargo-tests.reusable.yaml` (6 jobs fixed) - `ci.yaml` (2 jobs fixed) - `wasm-pack-tests.reusable.yaml` (1 job fixed) - `webview-tests.reusable.yaml` (3 jobs fixed) 2. **Added `shared-key`** configuration to improve cache sharing between similar jobs: - `linux-cargo` for Linux cargo test jobs - `macos-cargo` for macOS cargo test jobs - `windows-cargo` for Windows cargo test jobs - `linux-wasm` for WASM build jobs - `linux-msrv` for MSRV build jobs ### Expected Result After this fix, the cache key will use the correct Rust version from `rust-toolchain.toml`, resulting in stable cache keys that persist across CI runs until dependencies or the Rust version are actually changed. --- [Slack Thread](https://gloo-global.slack.com/archives/C09F3QMJE9G/p1770256299798159?thread_ts=1770256299.798159&cid=C09F3QMJE9G)

 

## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Chores** * Enhanced continuous integration build caching infrastructure by reorganizing workflow execution steps to ensure Rust toolchain versions are accurately reflected in cache keys. Implemented environment-specific shared cache configurations for Linux, macOS, Windows, and WebAssembly testing pipelines, improving cache hit rates and reducing overall build times. Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: hellovai

commit.message:
Rename crates to namespace conventions and add link_crates graph feature (#3079)

## Summary
- Renames crates to align with namespace conventions (e.g., `baml_lsp` →
`lsp_server`, `baml_ide` → `lsp_actions`, `sys_llm` → `llm_ops`,
`bex_jinja_runtime` → `llm_jinja`, `bex_llm_types` → `llm_types`,
`baml_bridge_cffi` → `bridge_cffi`)
- Re-exports `baml_base` types from `baml_type` to reduce direct
dependencies
- Adds `link_crates` feature to `cargo-stow` dependency graph:
namespaces can declare commonly-depended-on crates that get proxy nodes
(dashed border) inside consuming clusters, reducing cross-namespace edge
clutter
- Removes `concentrate=true` from graphviz output (incompatible with
proxy nodes inside clusters)

## Test plan
- [ ] `cargo stow --check` passes
- [ ] `cargo stow --graph /tmp/deps.svg` generates valid SVG with proxy
nodes for link crates
- [ ] Verify proxy nodes appear with dashed borders and original
namespace colors
- [ ] Verify proxy-to-real edges are dashed with consuming namespace
color
- [ ] All existing tests pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: fix vercel build, show last known prompt if there's errors (#3077) > [!NOTE] > **Medium Risk** > Touches CI/toolchain versions and caching behavior (Rust/WASM, GitHub Actions, Turbo), which can affect build reproducibility; app-side changes are mostly additive fallbacks for error states. > > **Overview** > Fixes playground UX during compilation failures by **caching the last error-free functions list** (`lastValidFunctionsAtom`) and falling back to it when the current runtime returns an empty function set due to diagnostics errors; the navigation coordinator also uses this fallback to keep selection/sidebar working. > > Improves prompt preview error handling by **showing the last known rendered prompt** when there are compilation errors (with a small banner indicating error count), and only showing the full error view when no stale preview exists. > > Stabilizes CI/Vercel builds by bumping Rust/WASM tooling (`rust-toolchain` to `1.93.0`, `wasm-pack` to `0.14.0`, `wasm-bindgen-cli` to `0.2.105`), ensuring `rustup default stable` on Vercel, adjusting Turbo dependencies away from a removed `typecheck` script, and tweaking GitHub Actions rust cache defaults/usage (including disabling `cache-on-failure` in `primary.yml`). > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 53f646e427dec230e3f9f36bd7c279a7af8ecdb1. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot). ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Functions list now persists when compilation errors occur. * Prompt preview shows the last valid preview and an error banner when diagnostics appear. * **Bug Fixes** * Improved error handling and display in prompt preview with clearer fallback behavior. * **Chores** * Rust toolchain updated to 1.93.0 and related tooling versions bumped. * Removed dev typecheck script. Cursor Bugbot found 1 potential issue for commit 53f646e

readme_html:
BAML: Basically a Made-up Language

Homepage | Docs | BAML AI Chat | Discord

BAML is a simple prompting language for building reliable AI workflows and agents.

BAML makes prompt engineering easy by turning it into schema engineering -- where you mostly focus on the models of your prompt -- to get more reliable outputs. You don't need to write your whole app in BAML, only the prompts! You can wire-up your LLM Functions in any language of your choice! See our quickstarts for Python, TypeScript, Ruby and Go, and more.

BAML comes with all batteries included -- with full typesafety, streaming, retries, wide model support, even when they don't support native tool-calling APIs

Try BAML: Prompt Fiddle • Interactive App Examples

The core BAML principle: LLM Prompts are functions

The fundamental building block in BAML is a function. Every prompt is a function that takes in parameters and returns a type.

function ChatAgent(message: Message[], tone: "happy" | "sad") -> string

Every function additionally defines which models it uses and what its prompt is.

function ChatAgent(message: Message[], tone: "happy" | "sad") -> StopTool | ReplyTool {
    client "openai/gpt-4o-mini"

    prompt #"
        Be a {{ tone }} bot.

        {{ ctx.output_format }}

        {% for m in message %}
        {{ _.role(m.role) }}
        {{ m.content }}
        {% endfor %}
    "#
}

class Message {
    role string
    content string
}

class ReplyTool {
  response string
}

class StopTool {
  action "stop" @description(#"
    when it might be a good time to end the conversation
  "#)
}

BAML Functions can be called from any language

Below we call the ChatAgent function we defined in BAML through Python. BAML's Rust compiler generates a "baml_client" to access and call them.

from baml_client import b
from baml_client.types import Message, StopTool

messages = [Message(role="assistant", content="How can I help?")]

while True:
  print(messages[-1].content)
  user_reply = input()
  messages.append(Message(role="user", content=user_reply))
  tool = b.ChatAgent(messages, "happy")
  if isinstance(tool, StopTool):
    print("Goodbye!")
    break
  else:
    messages.append(Message(role="assistant", content=tool.response))

You can write any kind of agent or workflow using chained BAML functions. An agent is a while loop that calls a Chat BAML Function with some state.

And if you need to stream, add a couple more lines:

stream = b.stream.ChatAgent(messages, "happy")
# partial is a Partial type with all Optional fields
for tool in stream:
    if isinstance(tool, StopTool):
      ...
    
final = stream.get_final_response()

And get fully type-safe outputs for each chunk in the stream.

Test prompts 10x faster, right in your IDE

BAML comes with native tooling for VSCode (jetbrains + neovim coming soon).

Visualize full prompt (including any multi-modal assets), and the API request. BAML gives you full transparency and control of the prompt.

Using AI is all about iteration speed.

If testing your pipeline takes 2 minutes, you can only test 10 ideas in 20 minutes.

If you reduce it to 5 seconds, you can test 240 ideas in the same amount of time.

The playground also allows you to run tests in parallel -- for even faster iteration speeds 🚀.

No need to login to websites, and no need to manually define json schemas.

Enable reliable tool-calling with any model

BAML works even when the models don't support native tool-calling APIs. We created the SAP (schema-aligned parsing) algorithm to support the flexible outputs LLMs can provide, like markdown within a JSON blob or chain-of-thought prior to answering. Read more about SAP

With BAML, your structured outputs work in Day-1 of a model release. No need to figure out whether a model supports parallel tool calls, or whether it supports recursive schemas, or anyOf or oneOf etc.

See it in action with: Deepseek-R1 and OpenAI O1.

Switch from 100s of models in a couple lines

function Extract() -> Resume {
+  client openai/o3-mini
  prompt #"
    ....
  "#
}

Retry policies • fallbacks • model rotations. All statically defined. Want to do pick models at runtime? Check out the Client Registry.

We support: OpenAI • Anthropic • Gemini • Vertex • Bedrock • Azure OpenAI • Anything OpenAI Compatible (Ollama, OpenRouter, VLLM, LMStudio, TogetherAI, and more)

Build beautiful streaming UIs

BAML generates a ton of utilities for NextJS, Python (and any language) to make streaming UIs easy.

BAML's streaming interfaces are fully type-safe. Check out the Streaming Docs, and our React hooks

Fully Open-Source, and offline

  • 100% open-source (Apache 2)
  • 100% private. AGI will not require an internet connection, neither will BAML
    • No network requests beyond model calls you explicitly set
    • Not stored or used for any training data
  • BAML files can be saved locally on your machine and checked into Github for easy diffs.
  • Built in Rust. So fast, you can't even tell it's there.

BAML's Design Philosophy

Everything is fair game when making new syntax. If you can code it, it can be yours. This is our design philosophy to help restrict ideas:

  • 1: Avoid invention when possible
    • Yes, prompts need versioning — we have a great versioning tool: git
    • Yes, you need to save prompts — we have a great storage tool: filesystems
  • 2: Any file editor and any terminal should be enough to use it
  • 3: Be fast
  • 4: A first year university student should be able to understand it

Why a new programming language

We used to write websites like this:

def home():
    return "<button onclick=\"() => alert(\\\"hello!\\\")\">Click</button>"

And now we do this:

function Home() {
  return <button onClick={() => setCount(prev => prev + 1)}>
          {count} clicks!
         </button>
}

New syntax can be incredible at expressing new ideas. Plus the idea of maintaining hundreds of f-strings for prompts kind of disgusts us 🤮. Strings are bad for maintainable codebases. We prefer structured strings.

The goal of BAML is to give you the expressiveness of English, but the structure of code.

Full blog post by us.

Conclusion

As models get better, we'll continue expecting even more out of them. But what will never change is that we'll want a way to write maintainable code that uses those models. The current way we all just assemble strings is very reminiscent of the early days PHP/HTML soup in web development. We hope some of the ideas we shared today can make a tiny dent in helping us all shape the way we all code tomorrow.

FAQ
Do I need to write my whole app in BAML? Nope, only the prompts! BAML translates definitions into the language of your choice! Python, TypeScript, Ruby and more.
Is BAML stable? Yes, many companies use it in production! We ship updates weekly!
Why a new language? Jump to section

Contributing

Checkout our guide on getting started

Citation

You can cite the BAML repo as follows:

@software{baml,
  author = {Boundary ML},
  title = {BAML},
  url = {https://github.com/boundaryml/baml},
  year = {2024}
}

Made with ❤️ by Boundary

HQ in Seattle, WA

P.S. We're hiring for software engineers that love rust. Email us or reach out on discord!
